string.text: I don't lend money!
Hey, Shroud.
What do you want?
So, what's up with you?
Halt, don't move! Who are you?
We're contract scientists...
Don't you know anything about orders? Documents for inspection! Slowly. If you try anything I'll shoot without warning!
Here you go.
Alright, let's see... The permit and pass are not expired. Stamp and signatures are in place. Everything seems to be in order... But why are you on foot? Where is your escort?
The car broke down, he stayed with it. It's not far from here, a couple of kilometers. We decided to walk since we can't go any further by car anyway.
When will they replace those pieces of junk? What are they thinking, those staff rats? They should spend a day here... Anyway, here's your papers. You may pass.
Thank you.
Hold it! Did you smear ink on your hand? Stop! Don't hide the pass. Yeah, this stamp is smudged, it's a forgery! Well, start praying scum!
Wait, don't shoot!!!
Tell me about the Zone.

I know! It's better to ask the eggheads about it: they can examine it under a microscope and stick it in an x-ray machine. I know a little about the Zone... It allows me to earn enough money so that in two years I can buy my own plot with a vineyard by the sea - and that's that.

Thanks, bro! You saved me, I'll never forget it! Sorry, I don't have any money right now. But I'll pay you back later! Let me give you the coordinates of the stash for now. I left some extra gear there from my last trip, whatever you find there is yours. You saved my life Shroud, I'm in your debt.
Well, anyone would do the same in my place.

Not everyone would risk their life for someone they barely know, belive me. In the Zone, it's every man for himself. Anyway, I'm heading to the vehicle station, I have some matters to attend to there, I'll contact you later. Take care and stay healthy!
You too!
There you are. We've searched the entire camp. So, alive, intact and kicking?
Yeah. Just got acquainted with our new friends here.

Are you talking about Cold by any chance? Well, we've known each other for a long time, but haven't seen each other in a few years. Nice reunion. So, shall we go to the village and discuss things? I found something in Logopedist's notes that I think you'll find interesting. By the way, here are your things - I can't carry them forever.
Let's move quickly, I'm starving and I have no strength left.
Thank God everything turned out fine. Those bandits could've easily finished you off, but let's not dwell on that stuff. I've learned a lot about our thing.
So, what did you discover?

There's an old man here, obsessed with clocks. They say he's been living in this village since the beginning, even before the first disaster in '86, can you imagine? And he survived the second disaster too.
And how can he help us?

Well, there were some drawings of old access points in Logopedist's notebook: Pipes, tunnels, ventilation shafts. I did some calculations and it turns out that one of these tunnels pass right under Watchmaker's house.
What are you suggesting? Break into the old man's house and search it?
Are you crazy? Why break in? We'll ask the old man politely. Maybe he needs something and we can bring it to him. So, will you go and talk to Watchmaker?
Why me? I haven't been getting along well with old folks lately...

I need to visit Shaman and give him Logopedist's notebook. It contains a lot of useful information for Stalkers. Don't worry, I deleted all the information about the catacombs from the notebook. So now no one else knows about these underground passages beside us.
Alright, I'll go and talk to him.
A lot of interesting stuff... I've found out where the Freedom member was captured. The bandits caught him right near the village!
Right here?!

Almost. There's a shack on the hill, that's where they got him. Can you believe it? The bandits have never come this far into Stalker territory before. Seems like they were tracking the Freedom member for a long time.
What's the shack for?
Just a regular shack, who knows why they put it there. Maybe it's for ventilation or something? It's impossible to find out though, the door is welded shut.
So, what should we do?
Well, this is where it gets interesting. Do you know Watchmaker?
No, who's that?
There you are. So, did you find out about the catacombs from Watchmaker?
Yep. Not only that, but I also got the key to the room that has the underground entrance.
Excellent. I didn't expect it to be so easy to gain access to the catacombs.
Well, do you feel like exploring?
Absolutely. Let's go.
Let's move.
Shroud, are you alive?! Man, you're something else! How did you manage to fall?
Oh... I'm alive, just about... I twisted my leg though.
The clumsy mountaineer, heh! Wait! I heard something...
What?
Quiet! Follow me. Don't make any noise.
Go ahead, I'll follow you.
So that's how it all happened. What do you think, can we handle it? Can we find the artifact? We can make history Shroud.
I don't know. It all seems too serious and I don't like Sidorovich, he's clearly hiding something.
Oh, come on Shroud. Aren't you excited? We've already overcome so many hardships and now you want to stop? Let's do it!
Well... maybe you're right. If this artifact is really as valuable as Sidorovich says, then I'll be able to return home right away.
Well, it's a motivation alright. We all gotta eat right? I get what you're saying.
Agreed then, let's continue further into the catacombs. But I won't go to Sidorovich for the key. Wait here, I'll ask Watchmaker for his key, he must have it.
Look at you, dressed up all nicely. Sidorovich didn't skimp. He gave me some nice gear too.
Guess what, someone broke into Watchmaker's house and stole his access card. That means we won't be alone down there. One of those three who disturbed Sidorovich definitely went down to the lab.
Well, look's like we're all set then. Follow me and don't fall behind.
Let's go...
Here we are, in Cordon. Don't you dare try to be a hero, the soldiers at the checkpoint don't appreciate jokes. Approach calmly, show them your documents and move along. You remember the story right?
Want me to repeat it?
Hold on, let me check your documents. Crap, the ink hasn't dried yet. If it smears during the inspection, we're screwed. Well, let's try to be careful. Ready?
Ready.
No, it's fine. Remember, if something goes wrong with these fake passes, we'll end up straight in hell instead of the Zone... Although, I'm not sure which is worse.
Do you think they'll notice?

They shouldn't, but anything is possible. The fact that the soldiers didn't open fire immediately is a good sign. But if they get suspicious they might, and then claim it was justified. It's not uncommon here, even those with legal passes get killed sometimes.
But there's still hope, right?
There's always hope, but the odds are slim, as they say. Don't forget that you can't rush in the Zone. Every wrong step can be your last, sometimes a calculated step too. Got it?
Are there any other options?

Of course. If the soldiers aren't completely deranged by the Zone they might demand money, which we don't have. So, keep a straight face and don't let your eyes wander. Look ahead, but don't stare. Got it?
Got it. Well, shall we get going?
What are you looking at freak? Thought you were the smartest? I've sent a dozen like you to their graves this morning.
Don't shoot. Let me explain!
What can you possibly say? That you tried to infiltrate the guarded perimeter?
I just wanted to earn some money. Let's make a deal.
A deal, you say? Well, show me the money and then we'll talk deals.
How much do you need?

How much is your partner's life worth to you? How about 30,000? Fifteen for each of you, that's a generous offer right? He can be our guest in the meantime. But if you dare double-cross me, I'll cut off his head and send it back to the one who sold him out, got it?!
I'll try to get the money, how much time do I have?
Maybe you will, you better get your hands dirty though. If you don't mess up, I think you can earn even more than what I asked for in two days. Just don't bring any junk here.
I'll find the money.
I have the money.
Ah, here comes our provider. We've been waiting for you.
Well, then so be it. That means your partner will stay with us. We'll have to squeeze every last penny out of him.
Wait, please! Don't touch Borland! Maybe there are other ways to solve this?
Hmm. There are a couple of options! Either you give up your equipment as a donation to the military or you complete a couple of tasks "and we're even" as Sidorovich says. Which one will you choose?
Alright, take my equipment. I hope we never see each other again.
I'll do your tasks, where do I start?

Alright then, we have a missing sergeant and valuable documents on a PDA. The internal investigation showed that he was last seen near the northwest tower during a chimera attack at the checkpoint. We've been tracking that agile beast near the swamp for several days, but we can't catch it. Maybe it has a lair nearby. It's possible that the chimera took the sergeant with it. So, your task is to retrieve the documents and kill the chimera.
Damn, so you've decided to send me to certain death?
You're a stalker! You came to the Zone for certain death! So let's skip the chit-chat, or have you forgotten who you're dealing with?
I remember, I remember... Alright, I'm going.
As agreed, here's 30.000.
I changed my mind.

Nice job bringing the cash, but the price went up while you were away. Your buddy tried to leave without saying goodbye. He broke the sergeant's nose and tried to steal his rifle, unsuccessfully. So now it's not 30.000, but 40.000 instead.
Nice job bringing the cash, but the price went up while you were away. So now it's not 30.000, but 50.000 instead.
Ah, you... Alright, here's another ten. Are you satisfied now?
Ah, you... Alright, here's another twenty. Are you satisfied now?
I am! It's nice to deal with a smart stalker for a change. You are welcome here now, feel free to stay.
No, thank you, I've had enough.

Suit yourself. By the way, don't forget your gear - it's in the box on the first floor. Also, if you're ever near our base, you can drop by. You won't forget where to find us, I'm sure! Ha-ha-ha! I'll let my soldiers know not to touch you. So come by if sometime, we'll find some work for you to do. We really need our own stalkers in the Zone.
Noted.
But that's all I have! I have nothing else.
Halt! Drop your weapon! Hands up!
I'm here to see the colonel. I have an appointment.
Stay still and don't move. Take off your backpack and drop your gun — you'll get them back on the way out. The colonel is waiting for you in his office. Remember, no funny business!
Don't put my stuff too far away.
Don't you dare tell me what to do! Or do you want me to educate you?
That's enough, calm down!
Why are you so scared? What happened?
Man, help me out! I need money fast! Can you lend me 30,000? I'll pay it back, I swear!
Ha! Of course. You're running around like you're on fire. Alright, enough talking. Let's start earning instead, shall we?
Yeah let's! What do I need to do?

We have a hotshot here called Blockhead. He's a promising guy, but as impulsive as diarrhea. He wants to do everything faster than anyone else. He's in the front lines when it comes to fighting mutants, you can't go anywhere without him when searching for artifacts and so on. He hasn't been seen here for a week now, and has already visited Garbage. Thinks he's experienced, but he's still green.
Well, I just arrived from the mainland myself. Where is this Garbage anyway? Isn't it too early for me to go there?

Calm down, no one's sending you to Garbage. The stalker is already here, he sent me a message a couple of hours ago, saying he's returning to the camp. He said he found something interesting at Garbage and wants to bring it to Sidorovich, the local trader. But there's still no news from him, even though it takes about twenty minutes to get here.
What do you need from me then? To find him?
Yes. Preferably alive and unharmed. But if something happened to him at least bring his backpack or any other belongings.
Where should I look for him?
In his last message, he mentioned wanting to take a shortcut through the "electric train" our local attraction. Start your search from there.
How do I get there?
Calm down! Tell me what happened.

First, you need to cross the embankment. Since you're new here, it's better for you to go through the tunnel west of the elevator. Just be cautious, there are some guys there who collect a fee from everyone passing through.
How much do they charge?

It depends on who's passing by. Right now, they have nothing to take from you, so let's try to negotiate differently. Here's a bottle, no - two bottles of vodka, just to be sure. I think they'll let you pass without any issue if you offer it to them. You can return through the "electric train" - just hop on a wagon and jump to the other side.
Are there any other options? I'd rather not meet those guys.

Of course, there's another way - under the bridge. But even experienced stalkers hesitate to go there, let alone someone like you. It's so hot there that one wrong step can be your last. And even if you don't touch the fire, you'll still turn into a crisp the more you wait around. So it's better to go through the tunnel - you'll be safer.
Will the bandits definitely leave me alone?

Don't worry, they won't touch you. Are they complete idiots to kill the goose that lays golden eggs? We, stalkers, are their only source of income. Bandits don't search for artifacts, but take it from stalkers instead. These are the rules here - it's easier for stalkers to pay for passage than to get into conflicts.
Yeah, it's a grim situation. Well, I'm off.
Wait! Where are you going without gear? This isn't an amusement park. Here, take this novice kit, it should be enough for now. You can get better equipment on your own later.
Oh, right. Thanks for your help. I'll go search for your lost comrade.

We're in trouble! Me and my buddy got caught at the checkpoint - our documents were fake and the ink smudged while the soldiers were checking them. Now they're demanding money. Or they'll kill my partner. And then probably me too...
How much are they asking for?
I told you - 30,000, 15,000 each. So, will you lend it?
I won't lend it to you, but give you the opportunity to earn it yourself instead. When did they say to bring the money?
I have to bring it within 48 hours, otherwise... I don't even want to think about what will happen.
Stay calm, everything will be fine! But still, you shouldn't delay, who knows what they'll do to your comrade. By the way, is this your first time in the Zone?
Yeah, it is. Is it that obvious?
What did you find?
I found the backpack. Couldn't find the stalker. Honestly, I didn't have much time to search - the boars were raging there. So, sorry, it seems the boars got the better of Blockhead.
Goodbye.

Blockhead is alive! He arrived ten minutes ago. His eyes are dripping like faucets - can't say anything coherent. We sent him to the basement to recover. By the way you did great! Take this bonus as a personal reward from me. Also, I talked to a local trader while you were away. He's got some work for you. The pay is not much, but it's steady. So, if you haven't already, go see him. His name is Sidorovich and he lives in a bunker behind the village. If you have any more questions, feel free to ask.
What should I do with the items?
Just give them to Blockhead. Though to be honest, we shouldn't give him back anything and teach him a lesson. Well, never mind, he already punished himself.
Thanks for your help, Wolf. See you!
There's no one there. I found a torn backpack near the campfire. But it's empty. Looks like the boars took all the items. I didn't see the stalker either. Most likely the boars got to him too.

That's a shame. But don't worry about Blockhead - he came back recently. He's all scared and mumbling something. Well, it's his own fault. That'll teach him not to show off for no reason in the future.
That's for sure. So, I've completed the job?
Oh, right. Take this as a reward for your efforts.
Hey, Wolf. I've been to the "electric train."
Hey hero! I brought your stuff. You left it in the backpack by the campfire.

Oh, another one. Everything would have been fine if I hadn't been caught off guard by a charging boar while I doing my business in the bushes. I didn't care about the backpack then. I don't even remember how I ended up on that train car, the animals were going crazy. Here's the thing, while I was walking from Garbage every bush had monsters popping out of them, I could barely run. I decided to take a break by the "electric train" and then these boars showed up. Wolf told me about you. Thanks for going on the search, I appreciate it. You can keep my things, I don't need that junk anymore. I'll buy myself some really good gear - you'll all be jealous then!
I'm glad you're okay. Thanks for the gear.
No problem. Wait, take out your PDA. Here are the coordinates of my stash - I hid a spare rifle there. Take it, as a reward for your honesty!
Wow, thanks! Good luck!
Well buddy, did the explosion get to you? Sorry, my boys went a bit overboard. But hey, they freed you and got us some new digs.
Oof, my head... What a day. Thanks for not killing me, at least.

We were responding to Shaman's call for help and were tired of the swamps anyway, had enough of sitting in puddles, it's time to venture out into the world! So, we decided to move to your area, to Cordon.
Well, welcome then!
So you're joking around? That means you're alive. What's your name?
They call me Shroud. What about you?
Everyone calls me Cold.
Who are you guys?

Well, not many have heard about our group and its heyday, and now no one knows at all. Some call us the "Swampers" but in general, we're all that's left of the "Clear Sky" faction. It used to be a scientific research group. Used to... but it fell apart.
What happened?
Even I don't really know the answer to that. We still can't figure it out.
And what brings you here?
Sidorovich, I heard you sell information. Is that true?
Alright, forget it.
What can you tell me about Shaman?
500 rubles, and I'll tell you everything I know.
Alright, here you go.

Shaman is a righteous guy, a former special forces or something like that. He may not be on par with Wolf, but he's been trudging through the Zone for years and can easily outperform many stalkers. He's a true leader, and stalkers stand behind him like a stone wall - he won't abandon them in trouble and will share his last piece of bread. He's an honest, straightforward guy with a sound head on his shoulders - a real tactical genius. You'd be amazed by the operations he carries out! In short, he's a reliable guy, a true stalker.
Do you know anything interesting about Mark?
I know everything if you got the money. Give me 500 rubles.
Do you know Borland well? I'd like to know more about him.
That's an exaggeration, I know some things. What did you want to know?
For that kind of information, my rate is always 500 rubles.
Can you break up a thousand?

Contrary to the rumors circulating in the Zone, Borland wasn't a military man, although he did serve in the army like others. He wasn't a scientist either. Borland was a thirty-two-year-old professional race car driver. Deprived of the opportunity to pursue his favorite activity due to the chaos in the country, which also affected sports, Borland began searching for a new place in life. And soon he realized that he could only find it in the Zone. All his talents found the widest application here: precision of eyes and hands, perfect physical condition, a cool head, and a passionate heart. Borland can assess the situation on the fly. He has an impeccable sense of time and distance. He didn't find anything fundamentally new for himself in the Zone though - it was the same as beyond the barrier, only more dangerous. If the cards are ever stacked against him and he needs to get out of here, he will find a way to do it.
I heard something about a guy named Fang. What can you say about him?
Oh, Shroud, now you're asking about the right guy. Give me 500 rubles and listen.
All ears.

Fang is a super stalker, a member of Strelok's group, he was a fighter responsible for fire support. He was a skilled shooter and smart too. He modified and reinforced his exoskeleton so much that he could go to the Monolith alone. He disappeared somewhere a couple of years ago. Some said he was dead, others said he left the Zone. I don't know for sure myself, and I won't lie to you.
Can you tell me anything about Strelok's group? About Ghost, for example.
Give me 500 rubles.
Here you go.
Tell me about Wolf. Who is he, where is he from?

Ghost was a natural-born scout - he could stealthily infiltrate anywhere. It's no wonder he was given such a nickname. Unfortunately, he died. He went into the laboratory beneath Yantar and never came back. Ah, what a loss - he was a skilled stalker.
Information comes at a price Shroud. Give me 500 rubles.
Ah, well, of course. Here you go.
Wow, isn't that too much for a simple conversation? I don't have that kind of money.

Sergey Ekimov (Wolf) and his family were always unlucky. His wife left him when he lost his job. He drank heavily for a while. But one day, when his younger brother, Pyotr Ekimov, got involved with a bad crowd and was severely beaten, with his nose and arms broken, Sergey lost control. He took his father's old shotgun, found his brother's abuser, and shot him. After some reflection, the brothers ventured into the Zone. At first, Sergey didn't have a nickname, he was constantly referred to as "Gray" until one of the experienced stalkers made a joke and recited the rhyme "Gray Wolf". The name stuck and that's how Sergey became Wolf. Over time, he got more experienced and famous. So he became the local veteran in the rookie village, traversing the Zone inside out and was considered a legend of the Zone.
Do you know anything else?
I see. Well, thank you for the information.
Hey, stalker. Ready to go on a safari with us?
Yeah!
Then don't fall behind and cover the rest of the guys. Ready?
Let's go.
Can I join your clan? I think I've helped enough to be considered one of you.
Of course! I liked you from the start and after what you've done, I'm ready to accept you into our clan. What do you say?
Yes, of course. What could be better than the life of a free stalker?
Cold beer and hot sand on the beach? Never mind... I hope I don't have to explain the stalker's code to you?
Better tell me, so there won't be any questions later.

Actually, it's quite simple. To be a stalker means to be human. To help a comrade in a tough moment, not to be sneaky, not to commit treachery, to share a medkit or a piece of bread, to cover each other's backs in battle, to share a campfire. Also, our main enemy is the Bandit scum, who only parasitize on our stalker brethren. Our goal is to cleanse the Zone of these degenerates once and for all.
I understand.
Then I won't bother you anymore. Everyone present, congratulate out new comrade's arrival to our friendly community! May you be free from claws and anomalies, stalker!
Alright, I'll go about my stalker business then...
I'll think about it some more.
How can I join your clan?

Easy! If I see that you're a good person and can be trusted, I'll call you one of us. You know, they say a friend is known in times of trouble. Well, we have plenty of troubles right now! If you help us, consider it a test passed.
Got it.
Got a minute?
What do you want? Make it quick, time is money.
I heard your guys recently caught some Freedomer. Is that true?
Kid, do you even know where you've come, who you're standing in front of, and what nonsense you're spouting here?
Sorry, I must have mistaken. I'll leave now.
I'm here, did you want something?

Oh, it's you. I have an offer for you. Will you join our brigade? We're in desperate need of people. As a newcomer in the Zone, you can either join us or the Stalkers. Everything else is chaos: everyone kills whoever they want. But we live by principles and teach others the same, for which we take our rightful share. Garbage is under control for now, and now it's time for Cordon. We've already reclaimed about half of it. We just need to clear out the stalkers in the vehicle station and show who's the boss around here, then we would completely dominate the area. As for the rookie village, it's filled with nothing but fools. So, it's advantageous for you to join us. Well, have you made your decision?
I've sent the coordinates to your PDA. You have a gun, so there won't be any problems.
Alright, I'm on it.
I'm a loner so I won't join your group. I wouldn't mind working for you though.
That's a shame, but I won't force you. I always have work if you're interested.
I'm listening.
We need to take down a stalker. He's currently returning from Dark Valley with valuable loot. If you kill him, you can keep the loot for yourself. Plus, I'll pay you extra. Are you in?
No problem, I'm in.
I've sent the coordinates to your PDA. You have a gun, so there won't be any problems. I'll be waiting for your good news.
I'll handle it quickly, wait for me.
No, sorry, I prefer to be on my own.
Too bad, kid. Well, up to you. Get out of here while I'm still feeling generous.
Alright.
Actually, I've always dreamed of being one of the tough guys.
Great, then you'll have to pass a little test, a real one involving blood. I happen to have a job like that.
I'm all ears.
We need to take down a stalker. He's currently returning from Dark Valley with valuable loot. If you kill him, you can keep the loot for yourself.
No problem, I'm in.
I killed the stalker and kept the loot for myself.
Job done, you can celebrate! Here's a small cash reward for you.
If you need anything else, feel free to ask.
I killed the stalker and took his loot for myself.

You did the job well, no questions there. But everything went a bit too smoothly... It seems suspicious to me. So, you hang around the Zone while I gather some information. If everything checks out, I'll contact you on your PDA.
Understood.
I need medical help.
Alright, let me take a look...
[continue...]
There you go, all good now.
Let’s have a look... Well, your liver is bigger than I'd expect, but that's an occupational hazard, right? Apart from that, you’re as healthy as a burer.
Hey, dead man! Empty your pockets and hand over your loot.
I don't understand! What's with the hostility?
There's nothing to understand, give me your loot or you'll die!
Alright, here you go. I only have this artifact and 1300 rubles. You won't get away with this though.
Get lost while you're still alive.
Hold on and don't move. Who are you?
I'm from Sidorovich, I brought the payment.
Got it. Proceed.
Here, take the bag. The seal is intact, just as we agreed. You can count it if you want.
Great. Let me check the number... All good. I won't bother counting it - Sidorovich always keeps his word. So, are you coming with us or will you wait here?
You go ahead, we'll see how it goes. Don't worry, I won't get in your way.
How's it going with Shmyga?
You arrived just in time - the mercenaries are already on their way. Did you rest up?
I'll manage. So, can I go now?
Hold on. If everything goes smoothly, you'll receive a whopping 10,000 rubles from me. It's worth it for such a job. Alright, now go.
Well, Sidorovich, that's quite generous of you. I'm on my way.
We'll all sleep in the afterlife!

Great! So, here's the situation: Some mercs will be waiting for you near the camp of that scum Shmyga and his gang. I'm paying them good money for this job. Here, take the money bag. Give it to the squad leader. And let me remind you, no funny business! There's a seal on the bag and I've already sent the number to the mercenaries. If something's wrong with it, they'll shoot you on the spot. So be careful around them.
Would they really kill me?

Absolutely, Shroud. The Mercenaries are serious professionals. If they take on a job, they'll complete it in the best way possible. Otherwise, no one would hire them again. But if you come across them in the Zone, it's best to steer clear. They won't hesitate to shoot you if you get in their way. Understood?
Got it. But if the Mercenaries are like that, why didn't you ask Stalkers? They're closer and won't ask for as much money.

Everything has to go perfectly, and only professionals can be trusted with that. Stalkers are a friendly bunch, but they can't keep their mouths shut. Alright, enough talk. The mercenaries will be waiting for you near the rocks not far from Shmyga's camp. Just give them the cash, you don't have to join the fight. The main thing is to find Shmyga and take my case from him.
Are you sure there will be a case there? What if Shmyga has already opened it?

He hasn't opened it, I'm certain. It has a tricky lock that can only be cracked by one master in the Zone. But I've already informed him about the theft, so Shmyga won't go near him. The most he can do is hiding the case somewhere, but it will be nearby, always within reach. So if you don't find the case with Shmyga, look for it in the camp.
Be happy Sidorovich! I took care of Shmyga.
The case, where's the case?!
Here it is, here! Take your case, Scrooge McDuck. Hehe!
Oh! Praise the Black Stalker! Well done, Shroud! Here's your well deserved reward. Ah, now we can live it up!
Well, don't let me interrupt your joy.
About the bandits...
So, how did it go?
I took care of them.
Nothing yet.
You're not dragging your feet, alright.
Thanks a lot! Here's your reward!
Shaman sent me, do you need help?

Help? Yes, I do. You see, we've just set up our base and haven't had time to clear the area yet. The bandits are all over the place, robbing stalkers who bring me goods. At this rate I won't have anything to trade. Just yesterday, another Bandit squad showed up. Bold of them - they settled right in front of our base! Could you teach them a lesson?
No problem! They'll remember my lesson for a long time.
You know, maybe another time.
Can I help with anything?
Yes, we have a little problem. Ask our trader, he'll tell you.
Alright.
Phew, I brought your equipment. It's heavy.
That's great, Shroud. We'll set it up now. The important thing is for it to work.
Can I help with anything else?
Yes, Shroud, but later. For now, you can go about your business. Just don't go too far, your help will be needed tomorrow if not today. Everyone's help will be needed.
Got it. I'll be around.
What's the matter?

Sit down, it's a serious matter. Pathogenych told me about our contact. I don't know if Logopedist or Sleeper arranged all this, but it's become tiresome. It's time to put an end to these scumbags. Are you in?
Of course. What do you need from me?

Actually, almost nothing. We have plenty of weapons, enough ammunition and armor for everyone. But we're really short on people. As you can see, we don't have the numbers here in the vehicle station. I don't count the rookies from the village - they're all inexperienced. To deal with the Bandits once and for all, we need allies. The military won't help us, they're too proud. Duty couldn't care less about our problems, they're fighting with mutants. Freedom doesn't even know what's happening around here and they have some sort of neutrality with Bandits. So, it turns out that we're completely alone against this gang of thieves.
What should we do then?
I think I know someone who can help us. Can you go to Sidorovich for me?
No problem.
Great. I'll contact him now, he'll give you what you need when you meet. That's it, you can run along now.
Alright.
Hey Sidorovich, Shaman sent me. I need to pick something up?
Ah, yes. Here's your cargo. So, are you trying to make friends with the "Swampers"?
What? I don't know anything about that. Who are the "Swampers"?
Oh, well, if you don't know then I won't spoil the surprise for you. You'll find out when the time comes, heh. Also, be careful not to drop the equipment, there's valuable parts inside!
I'll do my best. Alright, I'm off.
Thank you so much, Shroud. I'll tell our people how you helped me. Go see Shaman, he'll reward you.
Alright, how about you? How are you doing?
Not too hot but I'll make it. The medkit did its job and I'm feeling okay. I might need to visit the medic to patch up some holes, though.
See you later.
Hey. I was starting to think you wouldn't show up. Did Wolf send you?
Yes. So, you must be the best hunter around here?

Correction - the only hunter. They call me Leshiy. Wolf has already filled me in about you. Listen carefully - everything I say is to be followed without question: if I say stand, you stand; if I say lie down, you lie down; if I say kiss a boar, you kiss that boar.
Are you serious man?!

Just kidding! Don't get all worked up. Try not to make any noise and follow me closely. We're going to hunt down a boar. There's one here with the strength of a bear. It's injured several people already. We need to calm it down. Ready?
It's still just a boar right? Show me the way.

We last saw the boar not far from the train tracks. There's a small hollow there - it's always damp even on a sunny day. The boar will most likely be there. When we get closer, you go around the hill on the right, and I'll go on the left. Once you spot the boar, drive it towards me - I'll take it down with buckshot. Stay alert, there might be other mutants around.
Let's go quietly.
Damn, damn, damn! That damn thing! It gutted me! It hurts like hell! Do you have a medkit?
Here, take this.
No, sorry.

Thank you, Shroud. Listen, I can make it back to the camp on my own, but I'm no use as a shooter right now. Can you take me to the vehicle station? Who knows what other creatures are lurking in these bushes.
No problem. Hang in there, Leshiy, we're almost at the camp.
Well, if it isn't Shroud.
Do we know each other?
Not personally, but I've heard about you. I'm Shaman. I'm sort of the leader around here.
Nice to meet you.

Wolf speaks highly of you. Now I see for myself that you're a skilled guy, despite being a rookie. Well, since you're truly experienced beyond your years and willing to help a comrade in a difficult time, you can consider yourself one of us. From this moment on, you're officially recognized as a licensed stalker. We'll make you an ID later so you can roam the Zone whenever you want. But don't mess with the military, they can put you against a wall with or without an ID. Well, congratulations, Shroud! Here's a reward for you.
Thank you, chief! I'm happy to help!
Borland! Glad to see you alive and well! How did you manage to escape from the soldiers?

There are good people in this world. Stalkers from the vehicle station helped me out. I knew right away that there was no way you could gather that much money in two days. And Kolyazin isn't a fool, by the second day, when the deadline was approaching, he came up to me and started asking who else could pay the ransom for me. They can always put you against a wall, but there is no money to be earned that way. So, I sent a message to Shaman. He sent a messenger with the ransom. Kolyazin is very angry at you, so don't cross paths with the soldiers anymore. Also be careful, you never know what might happen.
Alright, I'll see you later!
Oh, what a mess. I almost had the money too, just a little short. Kolyazin kept sending me threats on my PDA, he even wrote that they fed you to the mutants, and now it's my turn. Well, I panicked.
Yeah, it's an unpleasant situation. The colonel is determined to get you now, he's stubborn like that. But don't worry, we'll come up with something.
True, let's not dwell on these things. How are you doing?

I'm alright, just a few bruises. Nothing serious, I've had worse. So, I called you here to ask: is it possible for you to become my partner? I've been in the Zone for quite some time, you won't get lost with me. True, I was on the mainland for the past few months but you know what they say, experience doesn't fade away! My last partner decided to quit the Zone and went somewhere far away, maybe to the States or somewhere else. He got tired, basically. I was planning to go with him, but changed my mind the last second. I can't live without the adrenaline! So, I decided to come back to the Zone and find a new partner around here. Then I saw you, this idiot who's crawling through a hole in the fence, right near the minefield. Well, I thought, that's it! Both this idiot and I are done for. That's when I decided to save you. Now I see that you're not such a fool, you catch on quickly. I can teach you a few tricks, and you could become a great stalker. So, what do you say, are you in?

You seem like an honest guy, so why not? But what are we going to do? I've already seen enough of Cordon - there isn't much to find here. I don't dare to go further alone, so maybe we should go together? Somewhere towards the center of the Zone, huh?
Hold on, you want to go to the center with our ragtag gear? We should get some proper equipment and better weapons first.
Any ideas about that?

I'm always filled with ideas. So, here's the plan: I'll go talk to Sidorovich. He asked for some kind of favor, either to go somewhere or do something else. I can't refuse or he'll get upset. I'll try to negotiate some gear at a lower price for the favor. So, I might be gone for a day or two. While I do that, don't sit here twiddling your thumbs, go and see Pathogenych. They've got something going on with Shaman and apparently it's not going well for them. They might need some help. Perhaps you can earn a little something there.
Hey, buddy, how's life? How are you doing?
Where have you been? I've been looking for you everywhere.
You won't believe what happened while you were away!

Let me see. So... hmm... wow... Is that all? Very interesting... What kind of an artifact does the Monolith have? I've also never heard of a laboratory under Cordon. There are some service tunnels below, but nobody knows where they lead. Also, an undercover Monolith agent posing as a Freedom member? It's hard to believe!
Do you think the info on the PDA is true? I mean, about the artifact and the laboratory?

I think it's a waste of time. If there was an artifact, someone most likely found it and handed it over to Sidorovich or someone else. But I wouldn't mind checking out the laboratory. Imagine if no other stalkers have been there yet, we can gather so much stuff! It would be better than finding a mountain of artifacts!
So, what's the plan?

I'll try to get some information about the underground from Sidorovich. He's been sitting in his basement for a long time, he's practically one of the first stalkers, he must know something. As for you brother, go and see Logopedist. Try to get some information from him about the captured Freedom member. The bandits still don't know they had a Monolith guy captive. Try to find out where they caught him, maybe there's an entrance to the catacombs nearby. Just be careful, you don't want to mess with those guys.
Okay, I'll do my best.
Well, what could have happened here? Mutants gave you a hard time? Or was it a Bandit robbery?
Neither! Someone almost cracked my skull in the elevator, can you imagine?! Someone sneaked up from behind and hit me right on the head. It still hurts.
What the hell were you doing in the elevator?
I was looking for Van there, he was supposed to unlock the Freedom member's PDA for me. I found it but it was too late - Van was killed.
Wow! Who killed Van then? Was it all because of that PDA? Rumors about the suicidal Freedom member have been spreading all over Cordon, and now there's his PDA too. What's the story behind all this?
Well, I freed that crazy Freedom guy from captivity and instead of saying thank you, he put a bullet in his own temple. He had nothing on him except the locked PDA. So I took it to Van to unlock it.
What's on the PDA? Anything interesting?
Not everything is clear, maybe you can figure it out.
I heard you might have some work?
And I've heard they milk cows in Limansk! Ha-ha! Well, I have a few problems. Can you help me sort them out? I'll pay well.
Alright, I'll do it. I'll come to you as soon as I find out something.
Tell me, what's the problem?

Me and Shaman decided that we've had enough of losing people to Bandits, even Stalkers are tired of all these fighting. Not everyone can pass through the "Bakery" so we decided to make a deal with the gangsters. They agreed to let our guys through the tunnel without any trouble, and we agreed to pay them once a week, Logopedist was satisfied. Well, we sent our mediator with the money to the gang yesterday, but he never reached Logopedist. I have no idea where he could have gone - it's like he disappeared into thin air. The bandits say he didn't pass through the tunnel and didn't show up in their camp. It's just a stone's throw away and yet he vanished. We don't want to suspect the bandits yet, especially since there's no reason for them to kill him. Everyone would benefit from the deal. But yeah, our mediator is gone.
Why did you send him alone?

Well, the thing is we didn't expect any problems. I've known Logopedist for a long time, we crossed paths a few years ago. Despite being a Bandit, he keeps his word. Although he doesn't get along with Shaman - they had a big falling out a couple of years ago. That's why I handle all the negotiations with Logopedist, he's clearly not involved in this.
Someone's disappeared regardless. Maybe Logopedist has enemies who wanted to set him up?
Yes, that thought crossed my mind too. Maybe someone doesn't approve of our arrangement with the bandits, and I can guess who that someone might be.
Who is it?

There's this scumbag in their camp called Sleeper. He was always in favor of killing all of our guys, but Logopedist kept him on a short leash. It's possible that Sleeper pulled off something like this behind his boss's back. Here's the deal: Our technician Van has golden hands. He hacked into the PDAs of the entire gang's top brass a long time ago. They don't have their own technician, so they call Van whenever something breaks. And Van is no fool, he's already cracked their frequencies, meaning we can monitor their movements at any time. Take this PDA, I'll send you Sleeper's frequency. Keep an eye on him, what he's up to, where he goes and so on.
I tracked Sleeper down. He's an unpleasant guy.
Did you find out anything about our contact?
I do, but it's bad news. The contact is dead, the bandits got him, but I don't think it's as simple as it seems.

That's what I feared. Did Sleeper decide to set up Logopedist, or did Logopedist know everything from the start? But then why did they kill our guy? So many questions, no answers. Here, take this as payment for your work.

That's not all. Besides your contact and the bandits, there was another stalker there. Judging by the patch, he was from the Freedom faction. But he was completely nuts - I killed the bandits and this Freedom guy picked up a pistol from the ground and shot himself. Can you imagine?

No way! You're kidding, right? He shot himself! We haven't seen anyone from Freedom around here for a long time. What business do they have here? And I won't believe for a second that he shot himself in the head. Maybe they like to smoke some weed or some other hallucinogenic stuff, but to commit suicide? You're quite the storyteller. Well, here's 500 rubles for your interesting tale. Go buy yourself something nice. As for me, I'll continue dealing with more serious matters - the bandits won't get away with killing our contact.
You're not believing me Pathogenych, but that's how it happened. Well, suit yourself, goodbye.
I tracked Sleeper down. He's an unpleasant guy.
Did you find out anything about our contact?
I'm not a hundred percent sure, but he's most likely dead. I didn't dare to get too close, but I saw a dead stalker there. I assume that's your contact.

What if it's not him? Well, who am I kidding, it's probably him, poor guy. The bandits won't get away with this, whether Logopedist knew about it or not, these scumbags need to be punished. Here's your payment - thanks for tracking him.
Hold on Pathogenych, that's not all. Sleeper and the bandits have a stalker captive in that house. I can't say for sure, but I think the patch on his armor said "Freedom".
That's interesting! Why would a Freedom member wander so far from his base? We've never seen them around here before... Are you interested in finding out? I'll pay for any information.
I'm curious myself, plus we can't just leave the man captive with those scumbags.
Then hurry and rescue him before they shoot him. Once he's free, bring him to us.
On it.
Pathogenych, I don't know how to say this... The Freedom member shot himself.
What? What do you mean he shot himself?
Exactly how it sounds! I took care of the bandits, approached the Freedom guy, and he picked up a pistol from the ground and shot himself in the head. I didn't even have time to react.

Well... We didn't really need Freedom in Cordon anyway, still, why did he surrender to the bandits? Aren't they supposed to be neutral? And why did he shoot himself, was he high or something? Well, it's quite a story. If you find out anything new, come back.
Will do.
Hello, master of hammer and screwdriver! I have something for you - can you unlock this PDA?
You forgot about the soldering iron. Hi, give me your PDA and I'll see what I can do. Why is it locked? Did you steal it or took it from a corpse?
No way! I didn't kill anyone, he was already dead, the owner I mean. Can you imagine, I saved him from the bandits and then he just took a gun and shot himself.
Wow. Well, never mind, it's not my business. I can unlock the PDA, but it will take some time. Come back in a couple of hours.
Great, I'll be back soon.
Wait, you should talk to Shaman, he has an urgent task for you. I'll unlock your PDA for free while you're at it, deal?
Deal.
Have you seen Van? I asked him for a favor, but he disappeared somewhere. Strange.

I have no idea where he could have gone, he doesn't report to me. Wait a minute... Sergey just came back from a walk like five minutes ago and he mentioned something about Van. It seems Van was running towards the grain elevator and nearly knocked Sergey down near the bus stop. Check there.
Thanks, I'll go take a look.
Have you seen a stalker named Shmyga around here recently? Someone who looks like him? Or anyone suspicious for that matter?
Suspicious, you say? Let me think. I haven't seen him myself, but the guys by the campfire were talking about some strange guys at the abandoned elevator. Maybe your Shmyga is hanging out there?
Could be. I'll go ask elsewhere. Maybe someone knows something about this Shmyga.
Ask Gut, he sticks his nose in everything around here.
Alright, I'll go ask. Thanks, Wolf.
Hey, have you seen any suspicious characters around Cordon recently?
Well, every face here looks suspicious. Be more specific - who are you looking for?
I'm looking for Shmyga. Do you know him?

I vaguely remember that stalker. It was a couple of days ago. We were sitting by the campfire, and this Shmyga was bragging to everyone that he's going to get rich and leave the Zone soon. Then he disappeared somewhere. We thought he left. But yesterday, Roman saw him near the abandoned house across from the farm with somebody. They were arguing loudly so Roman decided not to get involved and walked away. You should go and talk to Sketcher at the vehicle station. That guy knows all the rumors: who, what, where and why. He's like a one-man grapevine. If he doesn't know, then no one does.
Thank you for the lead.
I heard you might have some rumors to share. Do you know anything about a stalker named Shmyga?

Oh, people say all sorts of things. I know your Shmyga - not a pleasant guy. But if you're looking for him, you've come to the wrong person - he doesn't report to me, so I don't know where he hangs out. But if he's in Cordon, there are only a few decent hiding spots here: the grain elevator, the house by the farm, and the checkpoint up north. Check those places, maybe you'll find him.
Okay then, thanks anyway.
Thanks won't butter my bread.
Here's a sandwich with red caviar for you. Good luck!
Don't worry, you won't starve.
What do you want, scum?
Guys, I don't want any trouble. I'm just looking for someone here. Do you happen to know Shmyga?
Who are you and what do you want from him?
I just need to deliver a message to him, just a couple of words. So do you know him?
Listen, messenger, you better get out of here before we rearrange your bones.
I'll leave, just tell me if Shmyga is here or not.
Are you not getting it?! I'll open another hole in your head if you mention our boss again!
So Shmyga is your boss?
That's it, punk, you're in for it now! Get him, boys!!!
Damn!
Stalker Shroud reporting for duty!
Oh, you're quick! Well done! Here's a treat for you.
Don't worry, Sidorovich, I'll take care of everything.
Happy to help! Okay, enough jokes. What's the matter?

Well Shroud, I'm not in the mood for jokes either. So here's the deal: There's a stalker here named Shmyga. Silly name, I won't argue. But that's not the point. Anyway, this Shmyga owes me a hefty sum of money. He volunteered to be the middleman between me and some very serious people who dwell closer to the center of the Zone. It doesn't matter who they are, but I needed their connections badly. Well, I ran a couple of checks on this Shmyga and he seemed like a decent guy. He did everything smoothly and without a hitch. So I trusted him - asked him to deliver the payment to those people for the goods. In the end, I got the goods, but the money never reached the clients. Well, luckily I always keep a backup stash and managed to settle with the suppliers somehow.
What happened to Shmyga? Did he die or run away?

Well, I thought he died too at first. But damn it, he's alive and kicking. They don't call him Shmyga anymore though. I don't know his new name. But I know for sure that he's lurking somewhere in Cordon - the local guys saw a guy resembling him recently. So, I have a very important task for you: find this stalker, but don't kill him, he might not have any money on him. Your task is just to locate him. The rest is my concern. So, are you up for the search?
What's the pay?
I'll give you ten thousand. Plus, a discount on my goods. Shroud, this is really important, agree to it.
Alright, I'll find your debtor.
Remember, under no circumstances should you kill Shmyga or his buddies. If he dies, my money will go to the grave with him. And if he escapes to the north of the Zone, we'll never find him again.
I haven't seen Shmyga himself, but I know where his camp is. He and his gang have settled at the northern checkpoint. His friends are really aggressive - they almost put a bullet in my head.

Well done, Shroud! You did everything right. As for Shmyga and his gang, don't worry, professionals will take care of them. Rest up while I have a talk with someone. Come back in an hour and I'll have a special task for you. Just remember, you may need to shoot, so stock up on ammo and medkits, you never know how things will turn out.
Great, can't wait to teach those bastards a lesson.
I took care of those bastards, Shmyga's guys. But he wasn't there. Looks like he managed to slip away. I'm sorry, Sidorovich, but they started it.
"They started it"? Are you a little kid making up excuses? Oh Shroud, you've ruined everything! Who asked you to play the hero? I clearly told you - don't kill anyone! And what did you do?
I told you what I did! They shot at me first! What did you expect me to do, stand there and get shot? I taught those monsters a lesson, next time your Shmyga won't mess with me.
There won't be a next time! Now Shmyga will go to the center of the Zone and good luck finding him there. That's it, my money is gone. Oh, Shroud, Shroud... I had such high hopes for you. Goodbye.
Uh... I... I didn't do it on purpose, Sidorovich...
I tracked down Sawyer and discovered the stash location. Here are the coordinates.
Excellent, Shroud. Here's your reward.
What will you do with him?

We'll make him pay! Just kidding. Though, our clan has rules and he broke them, so we'll have to punish him. We'll take him somewhere in the woods, give him a pistol and a single bullet. It'll be a lesson for him, if he manages to get out, that is.
Harsh.
Rules are meaningless without consequences. I can't let it slide, that's why I'm the leader.
Well, good luck to you.
Is there any work?

Ah, Shroud, come in. I have a job just for someone like you. You see, there's a rat on the ship. The guys' belongings have started to disappear. Suspicion falls on Sawyer, but they haven't caught him red-handed, and no one can prove that it's him. I need you to follow him, but quietly. Just observe where he keeps going, and then show us that place. But act stealthily and don't take matters into your own hands, we'll take care of him ourselves.
We should just deal with him and be done with it.

We'll have plenty of time for that. But the stolen items won't be returned to us that way. This Sawyer is a stubborn guy. I'm sending you the coordinates of his PDA. See where he's heading again. Just don't make yourself visible.
Alright, I'll do it.
Phew! Man, you scared me. Why are you sneaking up on people like that?
So, this is where you're hiding the stolen stuff.
Hey man, calm down! These are mine! Who are you anyway?
Shaman sent me to track down the rat who's taking his supplies.

Damn! Please man, don't hand me to Shaman! He won't have any evidence without these items. Don't turn me in! I'll give you money. How much are they paying you? Two thousand? Five? I'll give you ten if you keep quiet. Just give Shaman fake coordinates, it's the stash of Vasya February. I'll throw a couple of stolen items there so everyone will think it's him. Deal?
Alright, deal.
Here's the money. Get your PDA out, I'll send you the coordinates of Vasya's stash. That's it, we're done.
Take care.
Are you Sidor's courier? Did you bring it?
Of course, here's your crate with everything intact. It was damn heavy.

Thank you, you've done us a favor. We were planning to go on a raid with the guys so we ordered various things, but there was no one to go and pick them up and the time was running out. Take this bonus from me personally for the speedy delivery.
Wow, thank you! If you need anything else, just ask for Shroud. I'm at your service.
What's the matter?
Finally, you showed up. I was starting to think you disappeared. Heh-heh.
That's more like it. Enough chit-chat. Hand over the package.
I don't get the joke. Why did you call me?
Do you need more work?
Obviously, I came here for a reason, right?

Well who knows, maybe you came to visit an old sick man, brought some oranges or some other treats? You guys never give us traders the respect we deserve. Do you think our life is a bed of roses? The color might be the same, but the taste is completely different. I might just pack up and leave for the mainland. Maybe I'll go to Crimea for a vacation. Then you'll remember who Sidorovich was and what he did for all of you.
Got that off your chest? Can we talk about the job now?
Oh, forget it. I don't want to deal with you.
Then why the hell did you call me, you old goat? If there's work, let's hear it. If not, I'm out of here.

Hold on, Shroud, I'm sorry. I got carried away... I do have something for you. It won't require much effort - you just need to deliver a package to a stalker at the vehicle station. He will be waiting for you at the entrance. I'll pay you 3.000 rubles for the job. Deal?
Everything went fine. I delivered it in perfect condition. That stalker even gave me a speed bonus.
Well, since he did, I won't pay you anything.
What the hell, you prick!

Just kidding! Have a sense of humor like me! Ah young ones, immediately spewing insults... you don't understand anything in life, especially here in the Zone. Here's your money - I always keep my word.
You're quite the joker. Petrosyan would be jealous.
Of course! We can't do without a little humor here! Heh-heh-heh!
Well, well, we have a comedian in the basement. Alright, call me if there's more work.

What the hell Sidorovich?! Why didn't you warn me that there are thugs roaming around here? They took your package! Damn it! I barely escaped with my life, they almost shot me. Give me compensation for the risk!

Get out of here, you bastard! Did you think you could scam me like that? You think I would just believe your word like a little child?! That stalker you shared the contents of the package with, he works for me!!! You're a damn fool!
Sidorovich, I...
Get the hell out of here before I feed you to the dogs!!! You dare try to trick me, you scum?! I never want to see your sorry face again!
Well, I... Don't be mad, chief...
Hey there, wanderer. I have something to discuss, let's talk.
What's the matter?
Come on, open the lock already. There's no time to waste!
Now we're talking! Just a moment.
Well, what's in there?
Wow, there's a lot of good stuff here! Hold on, let's divide everything fairly. Say, which one do you want: an AK or a helmet?
Give me the helmet. People keep shooting each other around here, at least I'll protect my head.
Give me the AK, you can't go anywhere without a gun.
Here you go. Now, let's see. Which one do you choose: a Soviet underbarrel grenade launcher or a foreign scope?
Give me the scope, I'll find a good spot for it. I was the most accurate shooter in my squad, you know.
Give me the grenade launcher, love those things - one shot and everything goes boom.
Alright, take it. Oh, there are more guns here! Which one do you want: an Italian shotgun or a British rifle?
I heard you're delivering a package to the vehicle station. How much did Sidorovich promise you for it?
Give me the rifle, not a fan of shotguns.
Definitely the shotgun - it packs a punch!

Alright, there are a couple of artifacts here too. I think I'll keep those for myself, I've earned it. But you can have this suit - "Wind of Freedom". It's a good piece, I tell you. Freedom fighters know their way around light armor.
Works for me. Is that all?
Yep. So are you satisfied? You got way more than you would ever buy for 3.000, that's for sure. As for Sidorovich, you'll figure things out with him somehow.
Yeah. See you later.
What's it to you? Go on your way and don't stick your nose into other people's affairs.
Calm down! Watch what you say or you might catch a bullet for those words. I want to help you, and you're cursing at me? If you don't want to take my deal, just say so.
What kind of help? I didn't ask for your help.

Do you know what's inside the package, huh? I can see that you don't, but I do. There's thirty grand worth of merchandise in there! And Sidor is only giving you what, three? So here's my offer: let's split the contents of the package in half, and you can tell the trader that Bandits took the package from you. I can even cut you for authenticity. So, what do you say? Say yes! It's worth it for such a prize!
Why don't you go away!

Oh well! If you don't want to, then you don't. It's my job to offer and it's your job to refuse. Just keep in mind that without special equipment, you won't be able to open the lock on the crate. And as luck would have it, I happen to have that equipment. If you change your mind, I'll be waiting here.
Sure, you go find some other fool!
Hello grandfather, where can I find the trader? Sidorovich or something?
Ha-ha-ha! Grandfather?! Ha-ha! I can't believe it! Ha-ha-ha! Oh, I can't stop laughing!

Come on, man. I get it, I'm not a fool. I'll find that... what's it called? Artifact-smartifact. Anyway, I'll bring you your miracle crap. Just don't get all hot-headed. Wait for me, I'll be back soon.
What's so funny? Are you Sidorovich? I got your message, what's the job?
Hold on, ha-ha-ha! Oh, the youth calling me grandfather! Oh... What did you say you wanted?
I'm looking for work, like I said.
What's your name? Your face looks unfamiliar.
I'm new here, they call me Shroud.

Here's the deal, Shroud. Before I give you a proper job, I need to make sure you're capable. First, let's start with the standard procedure - bring me an artifact. Preferably something from the "Bakery," my lower back has been bothering me lately - I want to warm it up. For such a service I'll give you... one thousand rubles. Deal?

Are you joking or something grandfather? Because it's not funny. I need to find 30,000 in two days and you're offering me a thousand? You can't be serious. Let's do it differently: you lend me 30,000 rubles now, I pay off the soldiers and free my partner, and then I come back to you and work off the debt. So, what do you say?
You don't get it do you, kid? Either you agree to my generous offer and find me some fiery artifact, or you get out of here and I'll make sure you won't earn a penny in the Zone. Make your choice.
About the artifact...
Did you bring it?
I found an interesting thing here. Look.

Excellent, Shroud. Here's your well-deserved reward, just as promised. This is a good start, but you're not ready for serious business yet. Take a walk, explore the surroundings, talk to people. If I need anything, I'll send you a message. Good luck, stalker.
Take care, Sidorovich.
No, I haven't found it yet.
Then why did you bother coming here?
Just dropped by to see what's going on.

Sidorovich, it's like hell in there! I tried to approach from the side and almost burned my pants off. I can't get close. And I still don't know what kind of artifact you want - what does it look like? Anyway, I came back empty-handed. Give me another, easier task.
Oh, you fool... Sorry, but I don't have any more tasks for you. If a job suitable for a scatterbrain like you comes up, I'll give you a call, don't worry.
Alright then, goodbye.
I found something, here. It's a weird thing, looks like a cave crystal but glowing. Is it okay?
Listen, I'm not sure if it's the right one, but here, take this ball, it's warm. There was nothing else there.
I'm here to collect my order.

Here's your Steppe Eagle. It turned out trickier than I thought. Some kind of pistol buff showed a lot of interest, but I barely snatched it in time. See for yourself - a hand-crafted gun, components are all adjusted with unmatched precision. Ever wonder what a perfect sniper pistol would look like? Well, this is it. You owe me 2,800 extra.
Okay, here you go.
There you go. Nice doing business with you.!
I don't have that much on me.
Okay, I'll hold the gun for you a while. I hope you find the money soon.
I don't want to buy this.
If you refuse to collect the order, I won't be able to rely on you next time around. I'll have to include the risk estimate in the price and ask for a larger advance.
Still, I will not be collecting this order.
All right, suit yourself.
I'll think about it.
Sure, that's up to you. I'll hold the gun for you a while.
Here's your ultrahigh-accuracy Steppe Eagle. You owe me 2,800 extra.

Behold the Alpine - a real beauty. You can rest assured this pistol is one of a kind in the whole Zone. A unique model, very limited issue commissioned for a single special unit. It wasn't easy to obtain, I even had to involve one of my overseas associates. An expanded magazine, extra hard steel barrel, receiver made of extra strong polymers. Add the remaining 2,800 and this baby's yours.
Here's your Alpine pistol with an expanded magazine. Add the remaining 2,800 and this baby's yours.

Lookit, this is March. The pistol belonged to a mercenary called Wolfhound. A compact high-caliber shooter with short blowback and a lightened bolt. Wolfhound was one hundred percent scum, but he knew his weapons, gotta give him that. They say his favorite trick was to blow all four of his victims' limbs off and then his head - before it hit the ground. Anyway, that'll be 2,800.
Here's your rapid-fire March pistol. You owe me 2,800 extra.

Here you go - Cutter, a submachine gun. Let me say it right off, don't you mind the appearance. It's a brand new model, not in serial production yet. It's a prototype, fresh from the armory. The designers aimed to make a new-generation close-range weapon: a combination of high caliber, rapid fire and accuracy. So that'll be 8,000 extra.
Yes, here's Cutter, a rapid-fire submachine gun. That'll be 8,000 extra.
Here's a fine specimen - Carabineer. A true army shotgun. Capable of automatic fire while providing good anti-recoil thanks to a balanced, shock-free system. Add 8,000 and it's yours.
Here's Carabineer, an automatic shotgun. Add 8,000 and it's yours.

I reckon this is just what you need. Eliminator, a latest development. The whole batch was supposed to be shipped to Central Africa, but I got a 'sample' as a special favor from friends. An automatic shotgun with a large magazine and an excellent rate of fire. Yours for only 8,000.
Sure. Automatic shotgun Eliminator, with an extended magazine. For 8,000.

Sure. Behold Storm, a submachine and grenade launcher in one. This prototype is a modified version of a conventional weapon designed for special units operating in the Zone. Technical innovation at its best from our Slavic brothers... Add 16,000 and it's yours.
Sure. Here's Storm, a submachine and grenade launcher with an extended magazine, for an extra 16,000.

Here it is - GP37, a new-generation assault rifle. Accurate, reliable, and convenient with a built-in optical scope. It was previously owned by a UN observer. Their group was sent to the Zone to investigate. However, the situation turned for the worse... 16,000 and the rifle is yours.
Here it is - GP37, a new-generation assault rifle. The price is 16,000.

Just a sec. Here's a modular FT-200M rifle, the brainchild of Belgian gunmakers. Accurate, all-purpose, handy for both righties and lefties, comes with an optical scope and a grenade launcher. I got it from some unlucky UN observers... That'll be 16,000 extra.
Here's a modular FT-200M rifle. That'll be 16,000 extra.

Here's Tide. Based on a silent sniper rifle, it was specifically designed for military units to fight zombies. Tide yields rate of fire in favor of stopping power, allowing the sniper to pick off zombies one by one without disclosing his location. However, it then became clear that stalkers alone will be enough to stop zombies at the Barrier, so the rifle never reached mass production. This baby's yours for an extra 16,000.
Here's Tide, a silent sniper rifle with increased stopping power. Add an extra 16,000 and enjoy.

Here. This is an SVU2-A, a short sniper rifle with an increased rate of fire. I bought it off Mac, the legendary Freedom guard. He swore there are no others like it in the whole Zone. It's an exclusive item, at least in these parts. It'll be 16,000 extra.
Yeah. SVU2-A, a short sniper rifle with an increased rate of fire. It'll be 16,000 extra.

Sure, just look at Lynx here. Hand-crafted by a local expert. He's deceased now, so there won't be any copies. It looks just like any other rifle, but take it out for some target practice and you'll feel all the difference. It's got a perfect combination of accuracy and stopping power. Add the remaining 16,000 and this baby's yours.
Yeah, Lynx - a sniper rifle with increased accuracy and power. Add 16,000 and it's yours.

Look here. I managed to get everything off my list - a CS-3a with a helmet. The helmet is nothing special on its own, but it allows for installing a mobile tactical complex, which includes a high-quality night vision system and an active infrared sensor. Now, the CS-3a armor is a real beauty. Excellent protection from bullets and anomalies is a rare feature indeed in a suit as light and mobile as this one. The whole thing will cost you 32,000.
Yeah. A set of CS-3a armor with a tactical helmet. 32,000 in all.

Specially for you, a SEVA suit. Everything just like you ordered - a closed-cycle respiratory module, thermal resistant, protective electrical circuit, and outer-surface anticorrosion materials. Plus several built-in artifact containers as a bonus. A must-have suit for any stalker. Toss in 24,000 like we agreed and it's yours.
Here. A SEVA suit. The price is 24,000, like we agreed.

There it is, a combat exoskeleton as large as life. The best protection you can get in the Zone from bullets, claws or grenade fragments, with the exception of armored vehicles. But it also takes after armored vehicles in that it relieves your weight burden - within reason, of course. 48,000 and it's yours, warts and all... err... by that I mean batteries and servomotors.
There it is, a combat exoskeleton as large as life. 48,000 and it's yours.
I want to place an order.
No problem. What do you need?
A pistol.
Of course. It'll be 3,500, of which I will require an advance payment of 700.
Okay, here you go.
I'll get in touch with you as soon as the order is ready.
I don't have the money on me.
Well, please come back when you've got the cash.
You know, I've changed my mind.
Fine. If you finally decide to make an order, I'm always here.
You're not a very reliable customer, so the pistol will cost you 4,200. And I will require an advance payment of 1,400.
I'm afraid the best pistols have already sold out. If you want the regular stuff, Owl's the man to see.
A close-range weapon.
Sure thing. The price is 10,000, and I'll need 2,000 of that in advance.
You refused to pick up your last order... So this one will cost you 12,000, and I'll need 4,000 of that in advance.
Hrm... You know, I probably can't help you there. The demand is too high.
An assault weapon.
Something high-caliber and with rapid fire capability, okay... The regular price is 20,000, with a 4,000 advance payment. What do you say?
You're a tough customer. I'll have to charge you 24,000 and I need 8,000 paid in advance. Deal?
Right now is a bad time... the military are making things hot at the Cordon. Someone attacked their outpost the other day, which made them even more difficult to deal with.
A sniper weapon.
For you I can get the best for only 20,000, but you'll have to pay me 4,000 in advance.
Working with you entails a certain amount of risk... I can try to arrange your order for 24,000, but you'll need to make a 8,000 down payment.
Sorry, this type of weapon is hard to come by right now. My contact for sniper stuff is... let's say he's indisposed.
An armored suit.
Yeah, I can think of something... suitable. It'll cost you 40,000, but you'll have to pay me 8,000 in advance.
Yeah, I can think of something... suitable. You refused to collect your last order, so the price will be 48,000 and you'll have to pay me 16,000 in advance.
I won't be able to do any better than the last suit I got for you. That one was both unique and top of the line.
A suit with a closed-cycle respiratory module.
That's a tall order... Won't be easy to obtain, not by a long shot. I can try to get one of these babies for 30,000, but I have to ask for a down payment of 6,000.
You let me down on the last order, so I have to think twice this time. I can try to get one of these babies for 36,000, but I have to ask for a down payment of 12,000.
I bent over backwards to get the last one. Don't think I'll be able to repeat that.
An exoskeleton.

You must have given this choice a lot of thought. I can get my hands on a combat version, but it'll cost you 60,000. Expensive, I know, but well worth it. Keep in mind I'll need 12,000 of that paid right now.
You didn't treat me very nicely on the last order, so this one'll set you back 72,000. Keep in mind I'll need 24,000 of that paid right now.
No way. Since they closed the manufacturing plant, vacant exoskeletons are typically found only on corpses... That's just not my method.
Nothing right now.
Sorry, my shop is closed at the moment. Temporary difficulties with order shipments...

Hello, I'm Sketcher. People usually come to me for unique weapons. Rare models, prototypes, personalized guns, and elite gear - as well as the latest developments in protective and combat suits... Each item is personally inspected by me, so quality is guaranteed! I also buy weapons in any condition at a low price, trade information, and can purchase useful intel.
Hello. Do you want to place an order or sell something?
Hello.
Your order will be completed soon.
Your order is complete. You can pick it up.
Can you answer a couple of questions?
Go ahead and ask.
Tell me about yourself.
Tell me about the Zone.
Ask away, I'll share what I know.
Well, from the first day here, one question has been on my mind: is it true that the Zone is constantly expanding?

What do you think? Of course, it's true. The Zone used to be much smaller. When it first appeared, its border was north of the current Bar area. I can't tell you exactly where, and no one can, but judging by the presence of anomalies, the Zone started around abandoned army warehouses.
And how fast is it expanding? There are quite a lot of anomalies here now, so was it less before?

Believe it or not, just a year ago there were far fewer anomalies here. And five years ago, there wasn't a single anomaly in this area. The military used to maintain a defensive perimeter around the current northern checkpoint at Garbage. But then the Zone started expanding. I don't know why, you should ask some scientists, maybe they know something about it. The expansion happened in spurts, without any apparent reason. This expansion was usually accompanied by emissions. They used to happen very rarely, once every few months. But with each passing year, the expansion slowed down, and the emissions weakened but became more frequent. Now there's a loud bang every week, but so far, anomalies haven't been seen beyond Cordon. Nevertheless, the military started building a new barrier, now about ten kilometers away from the current one.
What will happen with the current barrier? I assume this isn't the first perimeter?

You're right Shroud, this isn't the first barrier. Let me think... This checkpoint, the one with the wall to the south of Cordon, is already the third in line. They started building it when the Zone first expanded. Back then, many people lost their lives on the perimeter, some were caught in the emissions, but most were torn apart by mutants. No one had ever seen so many of those creatures; there were thousands of them! They sounded the alarm, and nearby units rushed in. Oh, the carnage that took place here! Artillery, aviation, tanks—everything was firing at once. They even made bombing requests over the radio, but by that time, everything had already calmed down. After that incident, the Zone was taken seriously. They decided not to rebuild the old barrier and hastily constructed minefields and fortified strongpoints to the south of Garbage. But they didn't stop there. They decided to build a second line of defense in case the first one couldn't handle it. The current checkpoint to the south is the former second line. Now it's on the front lines.
Why does the checkpoint look so strange? I mean, it doesn't resemble a fortified defensive position. And that wall, why is it built in a way that some structures ended up inside the perimeter?

It's strange to you? Well, it's not actually a checkpoint. There were no military constructions here before. Before the accident in '86, there was a service station. They even used it afterward to refuel and repair the equipment used at CNPP. And after the appearance of the Zone, the military settled in. There was no wall planned at that time. They started building it after the first breach when this checkpoint was designated as the second defensive line of the Exclusion Zone. However, the engineers messed up something in a hurry, or for some other reason, and they started building the wall south of the checkpoint. After the construction was completed, all the structures ended up inside the Zone. Some people got a good scolding for their mistake, and they decided to turn the wall around to at least cover part of the buildings. That's how it ended up like this.
And where are the gates? I didn't see them there.

Well, they're gone. They were demolished a couple of months ago when mutants made an unexpected charge. Interestingly, there was no emission that day, and it wasn't expected either. Suddenly, hordes of dogs, boars, and flesh appeared out of nowhere from the center, knocked down the gates, trampled a few soldiers, and moved further into the Great Swamp. When the helicopters arrived and started pounding the mutants, something really strange happened - all the creatures suddenly turned around and rushed back toward the center of the Zone. Nobody understood what it was, it was something never seen before. The gates at the checkpoint were not rebuilt since they will soon complete the second line, and then the soldiers will be evacuated from here. By the way, did you come from the south? Have you seen the second line? It's beautiful, isn't it? Everything according to fortification rules: flamethrowers, mortars, fortified trenches, and redoubts. The minefield is quite something; no rat can get through. They poured billions into it. Europe doesn't spare expenses on security, top-level sponsorship.
Thank you for the information!
Question 2
Answer 2
Thank you for the information!
Question 3
Answer 3
Thank you for the information!
Question 4
Answer 4
Thank you for the information!
Question 5
Answer 5
Thank you for the information!
Is there something specific you're interested in?
Tell me something interesting.
Have you heard the story about American spies?
No, what's the story?

Well, last year, Sidorovich received an offer to organize a tourist trip to the Zone for three foreigners. The money was good, so the trader agreed. But he's a cunning guy, he checks everything. He dug up information about these foreigners through his channels and found out that they were not tourists at all, but CIA agents. Sidorovich didn't want to lose the money, but he didn't want to expose the stalkers either.
And what did he do?

He came to me, explained everything as it was. We had a heart-to-heart talk, and we found a solution - to send Ilyusha Muromets as a guide with the spies. You know, the nickname suits him well. He's over two meters tall, with the strength of a bear. Once, he twisted the neck of a full-grown boar with his bare hands, just to see if he could. So, he can take care of himself if anything happens.
What happened next?

At the appointed time, Ilya met with the "tourists" and took them to the notorious places in the border locations. The "tourists" wanted to observe mutants from a distance, take photos of them, and if lucky, even shoot one. But Ilya didn't speak English well and understood only a tenth of what his companions were saying. All he understood from his limited knowledge was that the guests wanted to fight mutants, so Ilya decided to give them that.
I think I can guess what happened next.

Listen, that's just the beginning! Ilya dragged those poor Americans around for three days. They went everywhere and saw all sorts of things during that time. In the underground tunnels of Agroprom, they were attacked by a bloodsucker, and one of the "tourists" almost got killed. In Yantar, Ilya "accidentally" forgot to warn the foreigners about the zombies, and when a half-decomposed corpse started getting up just a meter away from the spy who was relieving himself, he almost relieved himself from the back too. Not to mention the encounters with several wild boar packs, blind dogs, and controllers that Ilya "accidentally" led the group to. And on the third day, on the way back, Ilya suggested spending the night in an unfinished complex in the northern Dark Valley. Of course, he didn't mention burers.
