I don't even know who was more terrified that night, the American spies who saw those smelly and dirty dwarves in tattered robes up close, or the burers who encountered screaming "tourists" on their ultrasonic frequencies. When Ilya was telling all this, we almost burst our guts laughing. But that's not all. The next morning, the "tourists," full of fear, returned to Sidorovich at the Cordon to be escorted through the checkpoint. The checkpoint was lively that day - some big shots had arrived from across the border. Kolyazin asked Sidorovich to arrange special conditions for the newly arrived. The foreigners turned out to be three CIA agents officially sent to the Zone for a special preparatory program. Sidorovich was puzzled why the CIA first sent their agents disguised as "tourists" and then officially sent three more agents. He shared his thoughts with the "tourists," who, with horror in their eyes and broken Russian, barely managed to speak. We learned that they were not spies at all but ordinary tourists who came to see the Zone and paid a considerable amount of money for their tours. They had no connection to the CIA!
No way! So, this Ilya Muromets of yours dragged around genuine tourists in the Zone and scared them in every possible way, thinking they were spies? Well, you guys really messed up! Ha-ha-ha!
Yes, that's the story. We almost ruined people's lives. By the way, we also sent the official spies with Ilya, following the same route. The burers in Dark Valley were delighted. Heh!
Thank you, you've made my day.
Thank you for the information!
Question 2
Answer 2
Thank you for the information!
Question 3
Answer 3
Thank you for the information!
Question 4
Answer 4
Thank you for the information!
Question 5
Answer 5
Thank you for the information!
Where are you from?
I was born in the glorious city of Okha, located on Sakhalin Island. I hope you know where that is? Nowadays, young people aren't interested in anything, especially their own country.
About Sakhalin? Of course, I know. It's an island in the Far East, not far from Japan. Well, well, how did you end up there? And what were you doing there?

It's a working town, mainly sustained by oil and gas industries. And there are traditional professions as well, like logging and fishing. So, there was always plenty of work. But since I was a child, I was drawn to adventures, I couldn't stay in one place. I explored all the mountains and forests around. But it wasn't enough for me. So, as soon as I turned eighteen, I happily went to the military enlistment office. In my case, it was the only way to leave the island and see the world.
Thank you for the information!
How did you end up in the Zone?

To be honest, I still don't know whether it was a chain of coincidences or if the Zone itself brought me here. In the army, I proved myself well. I've been into sports since I was a kid, so even two years in special forces weren't too challenging for me. Although, by the end of the Afghan War, I managed to get lucky and barely participated in combat. Then, based on recommendations from headquarters, I was sent for specialized military training. During my studies, I started a family. We didn't have any children, which turned out to be for the best. After my training, I was stationed in different garrisons, and there was no time for children. And then, I was sent to Chechnya. It was there that I lost my Marishka... Why, what a fool I was to bring her along. They warned us that the base was dangerous, that there could be attacks by Chechen militants. I was an idiot... I couldn't protect her.
I'm sorry, I didn't mean to remind you... I didn't know...

Forget it, it's my fault, my past, my burden. I have to live with it until the end. Maybe that's why the Zone brought me here? After the first Chechen War, other things have happened, long story. But I remember the emergence of the Zone as if it were yesterday. The chaos was unforgettable. No one understood what had happened in the first few hours: whether it was a reactor explosion or a meteorite impact, or maybe even the start of a war. Moscow was just as alarmed as Kiev. That's when our troops were sent to the Zone as agreed upon. I'll never forget that day, the Zone did its best. I lost almost my entire squad because of those rats from headquarters. They wanted to find out how anomalies affect people. Bastards. And no one even asked us. I can't imagine how Seryoga, Dima, and I managed to get out of there. It was as if the Zone itself guided us between the anomalies. As soon as I entered the headquarters, I punched that general right in the face. I was about to draw my gun, but they apprehended me. I would have been sitting in confinement for a long time if it weren't for my friends. They helped me escape from the prison cell. When I left the camp, I didn't have much choice: either try to survive in the Zone or struggle to exist outside world. So, I chose my path - I'd rather live a short time in the Zone than spend the rest of my life in prisons.
What a story... But don't you ever feel the need to go home, to the homeland?

You know, I didn't feel the urge at all at first, it wasn't on my mind. Nowadays, the Zone is somewhat inhabited, wherever you go, you'll definitely come across someone. But back then, there was no one here at all. Even though I'm a combat officer and I've seen horrors during my service, when you witness all the known laws of physics being violated right before your eyes and you see a loyal comrade disappear in inhuman agony, right in the air, there's no room for self-control. Fear replaces all senses. But you can't drink away your skill, somehow I managed to survive. Then, with time, as I gained more experience in the Zone, thoughts of the future started to come. Now, I really want to go back home. See my parents, if they're still alive, visit at familiar places. Believe me, I'm even willing to face a tribunal if they gave me just one day to spend in my homeland. Eh...
Thank you for the information!
Question 3
Answer 3
Thank you for the information!
Question 4
Answer 4
Thank you for the information!
I've already told you everything I know.
Question 5
Answer 5
Thank you for the information!
No, I changed my mind.
text in default
Rise and shine stalker!
What is going on?! Why does everyone keep hitting me on the head?
Be grateful they left you alive. Looks like Logopedist has special plans for you.
Damn! My head hurts, I have no strength. Listen, buddy, maybe we can make a deal? I can pay.
What will you pay with? You have nothing! And I don't want to deal with Logopedist later.
How much do you want? My partner has money, he'll pay! His name is Borland.
Borland, you say? I've heard of him. Yeah, he might pay. Alright, you sit here for now, I'll have a chat with your friend.
Where else would I go?
Damn it! You scared me, you wretched scoundrels! What the hell are you doing here?
Sidorovich?! What are you doing here?
Shroud, let's be straight with each other. I can see right through you, you can't hide anything from me.
Well, we... um...
Spit it out, don't drag it out.
Okay, okay. Have you heard about the deal with the Freedom stalker?

About the suicide? Of course. Whole Cordon is on edge, people are afraid that the Freedom guys will come here to investigate. No one can prove to them that he shot himself in the head after all, you're the only witness. So, what's the deal with that stalker?
He's not from Freedom. He was a secret Monolith agent.
Are you serious?! That can't be! What was he doing here? Their group has long since fallen apart, the puppeteers have all been killed.
What are you talking about?
I'll tell you another time. We don't have to be so secretive now though, so I'll share some information with you. But tell me about this agent first, you didn't bring him up for no reason, right?

Well, you see, this Monolith guy had a PDA on him but it was locked. I asked Van to unlock it, that was when I came to you for some work. When I left your bunker and went to see the technician I found out that he had disappeared without a trace. I went to look for him... and found him too. I wish I hadn't...
None of your damn business! So, it was you guys, I can't believe it! The alarm was triggered three times this week! Which entrance were you using?
Well, go on, what happened?
Van was killed.
So that's where he disappeared. The whole camp has been searching for him for a day now. Why didn't you say anything earlier?

Hold on, Sidorovich, let me finish. When I found Van, someone hit me on the head hard. I was unconscious for several hours in the elevator. When I woke up Van was gone, as if he was never there. Who would believe that I didn't touch him when there's no body?
Well, that's true. Wait a minute, where did you say you found Van, in the elevator?
Yeah, yesterday. So what? Do you have any ideas who knocked me out?

I do, but I can't tell you. Let's just say I arranged a meeting for some very serious people in the elevator yesterday, and the meeting got disrupted. Probably because of you and Van. But who could have killed the technician remains a mystery to me. Although...
What? Sidorovich, don't beat around the bush. Van's dead, this is no time for secrets.

Alright, fine, it's a serious matter. I sent a stalker to the elevator. He found an interesting container at Garbage recently, which my clients from the center of the Zone have been searching for a long time. The one called Shmyga stole their money you know.
What container? What's inside? And who is that stalker?
Sidor, what alarm? Three times this week? This is our first time here, I swear.

Well, who knows what's inside the container. I didn't open it, just looked at the numbers on the lid and immediately realized it was the exact container my clients were looking for. The stalker is called Blockhead. He was thrilled when I told him about the deal. I didn't dare to buy the container from him, so I arranged the meeting. But now I don't know what happened - the clients didn't get the container, the stalker disappeared, and you and Van are involved. My clients wouldn't attack Van - it's not their style, and I doubt the stalker would do something like that either. Although I can't vouch for him. There must be someone else involved. Maybe Bandits?

Blockhead? Well, well! I know him. Wolf asked me to find that stalker when we first met. He seemed strange to me back then, didn't care about his reputation. And look what he was planning. Maybe he killed Van and then hit me on the head before he was seen?
Could be, although the guy seemed normal, no strange behavior or anything. Why would he kill Van? It's truly a strange story. And now we don't even know where to find Blockhead.

Yeah... So, where was I? Oh yeah. I found the unlocked Monolith PDA on Van. There were only a few messages left. In short, it talked about a group of stalkers who stole an artifact from Monolith and were being pursued by them. The artifact has devastating effects on people, something about the psyche was mentioned, too complex for me. That's why it was put in a container, and the stalkers took off with it.
A container, you say? Could it be the same container I was telling you about?..

Maybe. Anyway, listen. During the pursuit several of the stalkers were killed, but the remaining ones managed to lose their pursuers. That's why Monolith brought in their undercover agents to track down the thieves. I don't know how many agents there were, but one of them, posing as a Freedom member, made it here to Cordon. He traced one of the thieves to the rookie village, but he himself was captured by bandits. I don't know how.

I don't get it. Are you saying Blockhead is one of those stalkers? It can't be. He's only been in the Zone for a couple of weeks and just recently made it to Garbage. He spent only a day there and came back. It was on that same day he brought me that container. I already told you what happened afterwards.

So, Blockhead found the container in Garbage. The way I see it, those stalkers who stole the artifact hid it in Garbage and Blockhead stumbled upon it by chance. He couldn't have killed them. Or could he? Someone killed Van. And then there's the artifact. What do you think, Sidorovich, what kind of artifact is in the container?

Well, who knows! New artifacts appear every day. But if I were to guess, there's one artifact that comes to mind with a similar effect, it's called "Pendulum". It's an extremely rare artifact however, only two of them have ever been found. They're worth a fortune.
And why is it so highly valued?

Stop messing with me! Who else could've triggered the alarm? It's designed for humans, not rats or dogs, and mutants don't roam around here. So, only a human could have triggered it. If it wasn't you, then who?

This artifact is two-sided. It has two opposing properties: its one side absorbs all psi-radiation, but the other side emits it with incredible power. The absorption and emission poles switch over a certain period of time, about an hour or so. That's why you need to constantly flip it when carrying it on your belt.\nBroker's group was the first to find this artifact. But at that time they didn't know the effects of the Pendulum. Because of this, only Corkscrew managed to reach the scientists' camp in Yantar alive. He could barely stand on his feet. The eggheads flew him to mainland on a helicopter, and of course, they kept the artifact for research. Several of their own lab technicians nearly died until they assembled a suitable protective container. I learned about this later from a copy of the report. Sakharov was involved. Since then, nothing is known about the artifact. It's probably still being studied in some secret research institute.\n
And what about the second one?
Oh, the story of the second artifact is even more interesting. Have you heard of Strelok?
No, who is Strelok?

Not just an ordinary stalker, but a legend! I don't know when he appeared in the Zone, but he knew his business inside out - the artifacts he'd bring, man, you'd be amazed. No one knew where he found them either. One time, he was lucky enough to find this very Pendulum, being the clever guy he is, he immediately realized that the artifact had a negative influence. I don't know how many days he studied this artifact, but he found out almost everything about it that the scientists have in their labs. And all in field conditions, can you imagine?! After that, he started carrying this artifact everywhere. And thanks to him, he led his group through the Brain Scorcher. They brought so much stuff that you could have bought a small island in the Pacific. Traders from all over the Zone lined up for his artifacts. But he was stubborn, he sold a few and kept the rest for himself, who knows where he put them. Ah, he was a good man.
Why "was"? What happened to him?

Strelok suddenly disappeared a couple of years ago. We thought he died because of the massive emission. I've never seen anything like that emission before and believe me, I've seen a lot. Anyway, to our surprise, they found him alive in one of the death trucks a few months later. How he ended up there is unknown. He didn't even remember anything, not even his own name. I didn't recognize him right away - it was scary to look at him: thin, covered in bruises, weathered face, mysterious scars all over his body. And that mark on his arm, as if he was branded by beasts - the S.T.A.L.K.E.R. tattoo. But the strangest thing was that there was only one entry in his PDA: "Kill Strelok." That's right, himself, can you imagine?
What did he do then?

I won't lie, we called him Marked One because of his tattoo and used him. He needed to find Strelok, which meant he could find his stashes as well. But the most interesting thing for us was the path to the north of the Zone, where the motherlode of artifacts is located. So, me and the other traders decided to keep him in the dark for now.
I'm not surprised.
Sidorovich, I swear we didn't mean any harm. We made a deal with Watchmaker and he let us explore the tunnel and catacombs. We didn't plan anything bad.

Ahem. So, Strelok, I mean Marked One, set out to find himself. That is, he followed his own tracks to the north of the Zone. The scientists gave him a prototype psi-blocker to keep his brain from boiling. Unbeknownst to Marked One, he was gathering all the information about the inaccessible areas of the Zone for them while wearing it. He also disabled two emitters: one in Yantar and one in Radar - the infamous "Brain Scorcher." The path to Pripyat and the center of the Zone was opened. It shouldn't have been done, but who knew how it would all turn out?\nEveryone in the Zone went crazy. Even the laziest ones rushed to the center. Everyone wanted to be the first to reach the "Wish Granter." I was happy at the time, thinking that since the path to Pripyat and CNPP was now open, the artifacts would flow to me like a river. But things didn't go as planned - no one came back. Most fell in Pripyat, the whole city was teeming with Monolith fighters. All factions started warring again - no one wanted to retreat, everyone was pushing forward. Later, the military also joined in. In short, it was chaos. And to top it off, an emission happened. The number of casualties was uncountable.
And Strelok? Did he make it out?

I don't know. No one has seen him here since that incident, we couldn't even find the psi-blocker. It had a GPS module but it seems the emission damaged it. The last coordinates pointed to the entrance of the Sarcophagus though, which means Strelok reached it a couple of minutes before the emission. Maybe he survived, we'll never know. It's been a year since then and many people have been to the center. They found a strange laboratory in one of the bunkers near CNPP - there were bodies in capsules, and it looked like they were shot while asleep. Stalkers found various documents there and handed everything over to the scientists. What was written there was unbelievable. But eventually, we came to the conclusion that it was all true.
What did the documents say?

It contained technical information about psi-emitters, generators, and Wish Granter. It turned out that the Monolith was an artificial construct used to brainwash fanatics. This explained why no one found said Wish Granter after the emission - there was nothing in the Sarcophagus except for the destroyed reactor. With the help of this device, certain scientists from the C-Consciousness project, as they called themselves in the documents, hypnotized stalkers who got too close to the secret of the Zone's origin. The recruited agents were given secret assignments and sent to the Zone's borders. Apparently, Strelok somehow became one of these agents and was tasked with killing himself. But then the Monolith was destroyed. The fanatics started to regain their senses after that - several dozen squads returned from the center, not remembering anything. Initially, they were almost killed, as they were former enemies of all stalkers. Not everyone has fully recovered from the hypnosis however, and judging by your story, there are still many puppets without puppeteers. They're up to something too, hence why the Pendulum artifact was placed in the container. I wonder if that's the same artifact that belonged to Strelok?
That's what Borland and I will try to find out. Thank you for the interesting story. We'll continue exploring the catacombs.

Hold on Shroud, I have a proposition. In light of recent events I suggest we make a deal: I'll provide you with top-notch equipment and weapons, and in return, if you find the Pendulum artifact you come and sell it specifically to me. I'll pay you handsomely, what do you say?
I don't know, Sidorovich. What if we don't find anything?

Well, in that case you can just pay me the minimum rental fee for the gear. The most important thing is for you to stay alive because searching for and stripping armor off corpses is not a pleasant task.
Well, that's reassuring. We need to think it over, consult with each other. Let's talk this later, okay? In the meantime, we'll explore this place and maybe find Blockhead.
Wait, Watchmaker let you in? So you didn't come in through the pipe?
What pipe? We used the stairs. What pipe are you talking about?

That's bad Shroud. You may act like a fool sometimes, but you can't lie to save your life. This means that three people, not counting you, have infiltrated here in the past week. So, you're saying you're here looking for something?
I'm not saying anything. We just wanted to see what's down here and how it looks. It's an interesting place, you know.
We've considered your offer. We agree.

Now we're talking! Hold on, let me grab the stuff. Here! Just as I said. It may not be the best equipment, but it's been tested on countless expeditions. You can only get something better by ordering it from the mainland. I'll send you the master code via PDA too. It'll work a hundred percent, unlike regular service codes. And finally, here's the rest of the gear, just as promised. Now, I'll let you through my passage. Are you ready?
Ready.
Tell me about yourself.

I value independence, so I've always been on my own and worked for myself. But when I heard that the stalkers had outpaced the military and established their own base, I came here right away. You know, I've crossed paths with many people in the Zone, but as far as I'm concerned, Stalkers are the real deal. Well, how can I put it... they're normal, you know? No silly codes, rules, like in other factions. In my opinion, the Zone should belong to the stalkers alone: then there will be mutual assistance everywhere, and it will be more peaceful. So, I decided to support these guys.
You rarely come across technicians in the Zone. Can you tell me what you're capable of?

Well, don't expect any fancy gadgets or sophisticated stuff from me. But you don't need to doubt me either: my kettle is still boiling and I have skilled hands. I can perform work that can save your life. I repair weapons and armor most of the time, but if the necessary parts show up, I can even upgrade your gun or gear. If you want to know exactly what I do, take a look at the upgrades - I think you'll figure it out.
I guess you've wandered around the Zone quite a bit? Have you seen anything unusual?

Unusual? I came here through Agroprom right after the powerful emission, and I saw some wonders... In places where there used to be nothing were now patches of twisted land that has swelled up. They were emitting such radiation that you have to run past it. The guys later told me that there's no more powerful anomaly than that one: it's not just an energy fountain, but a Niagara Falls of energy! It's a dangerously unpredictable place, but the catch is that rare artifacts often appear there, unique ones. So if you come across that place, keep an eye out.
Tell me about yourself.

I'm a trader... a rookie. As you can see I'm pretty new here, but as soon as Shaman said that anyone can become whoever they want, I decided - I'll be a trader! And why not? I have the skills and Sidorovich is a living example that it can be done... You know, back in school, I used to profit using an battery toy - remember, the one where the wolf catches chicken eggs? - well, I used to let my classmates play for ten kopecks per game. Oh, those were the golden days... I have a knack for it, you know? Now I'm settling in, learning the trade, so to speak. I'm taking goods from Sidorovich for resale, so the prices are... you know... aren't that great yet. But still! There are some interesting things to be found. The guys have started bringing me some loot little by little too.

Oh buddy, we're going to live like kings now, like we've got divine favor... or whoever's in charge of us sinners. The military won't bother us anymore, things are looking up for us Stalkers. We'll be able to relax on Cordon after raids and the equipment we need will be right there for us... We'll also get real money for our goodies, not like before, where we got scraps, and the cream went to the soldiers. We'll still mess with the military though! So what? They shouldn't relax, you know, it's not a fairy tale we're in! Long story short, we're going to live it up!
How do you think life will change in Cordon?

Oh buddy, we're going to live like kings now, like we've got divine favor... or whoever's in charge of us sinners. The military won't bother us anymore, things are looking up for us Stalkers. We'll be able to relax on Cordon after raids and the equipment we need will be right there for us... We'll also get real money for our goodies, not like before, where we got scraps, and the cream went to the soldiers. We'll still mess with the military though! So what? They shouldn't relax, you know, it's not a fairy tale we're in! Long story short, we're going to live it up!
Thank you very much, comrade stalker.
You're welcome "comrade." Man, where do you people even come from...
I'm from Novosibirsk. You know, Akademgorodok...
What's the matter with you?! Such a chatterbox! Well, I've done my job, others can deal with you now. Good luck!
Hey, Student. Ready to go?
So you'll be my guide? Nice to meet you. Are you an experienced stalker? Isn't it dangerous? What if we get shot by Bandits on the way? And mutants...

Shut up! Stop talking for a second! Oh, I have a bad feeling about this... Let's get one thing straight: if you keep talking on the road, I'll leave you behind. Then you'll have to find your own way out. Got it? Let's go.
Are you the new technician?
Yes, Van disappeared somewhere so now I'm taking his place.
I wanted to ask about tools.
What about them?
Well, I used to bring tools to Van...
Yeah, well, not all of them are here, so we still need more. If you find any, bring them over.
Alright.
I brought you some tools.
Great! Which ones?
Tools for basic work.
Excellent! Now if only you could find tools for fine and calibration work...
Tools for fine work.
Excellent! Now if only you could find tools for basic and calibration work...
Calibration tools.
Excellent! Now if only you could find tools for basic and fine work...
Nothing for now.
Great! Now if I could just get some calibration tools, everything would be perfect.
Great! Now if I could just get some tools for fine work, everything would be perfect.
Great! Now if I could just get some tools for basic work, everything would be perfect.
Wow! You're capable... where did you find them all?
If you need tools, I can get them for you.

That would be great! I feel like I'm working in the Stone Age. The only modern tool I have is a soldering iron, but even that's like a shamans wand... With the right tools, I can do top-notch tuning, and you'll get your fair share too.
Alright then. I'll look for them.
I changed my mind.
Great! I'll do the tuning, you'll make some profits.
Hi. Do you need tools?
Do you even have to ask?! Of course I need them. Show me what you have.
Tools for basic work.
Great! Now I can modify your weapons and armor to a higher level.
Tools for fine work.
Excellent! Now our local scientific and technological progress will take a leap forward.
Calibration tools.
Great! Now I'll be able to make you such a powerful rifle that even Sidorovich would give his soul for it, hehe.
Nothing for now.
Welcome to our humble corner of happiness, dear traveler. Would you like to see the menu?
What?
Show me the money, that's what!
Um, I don't have any money.

Heheh, you're funny! And where do you think you're going without money, you fool? No money means no gear, no gear means no artifacts, and no artifacts means... Hmm, what comes next? Oh right! No artifacts means no money! That's what we call a vicious circle, see? Heheh! So why don't you get lost while you're still in one piece! You can thank me later. You damn superhero you.
Guys, I need to pass, it's really important. Maybe we can work something out?
Hehe! He needs it! Maybe I "need" to sober up, but you don't see me bothering you with my problems.
Well, how about some vodka? I have some.
Guys, it's my first day in the Zone. Maybe you can let me pass, just this once?
Hehe! Look at you, "let me pass, just this once." Just like that huh!? Either pay up or get lost - there's no third option!
Damn all of you!
Heheh, you're a decent guy. Alright, give me your vodka. I still won't let you pass though. Haha!
I have another bottle - here you go!
Oh, look at you! You're a real magician, I respect that. Alright, you can pass through the tunnel. We'll take care of business with the gang in the meantime. Now get lost!
Already gone.
Thank you son. Mutants have been going crazy lately, not allowing anyone through.
No problem grandfather. You're Watchmaker, right?
Well, yes. Who might you be?
They call me Shroud. I've been looking for you.
Me? Well, how can I help you?
Tell me about the tunnel under the village.
You're straight to the point, aren't you son? I respect such people. I'll tell you everything I know, but don't mention it to anyone, got it?
Got it!

After the Great Patriotic War, Soviet leadership was extremely concerned about the security measures and escape routes for high-ranking officials. Therefore, it was decided to create a secret underground communication network. Primarily, this network was intended to provide emergency evacuation from besieged cities such as Kiev, Minsk, and Moscow. One of the underground railway branches of this network is located here, beneath the Zone, it's the Kiev-Minsk route. Along with this branch, underground communications were built to serve the rolling stock and deliver materials to other important facilities that were abundant at that time. I was assigned to guard this secret passage. That's why I'm still here, keeping watch.
So, the tunnel beneath the village leads to a railway? That can't be true!

Oh, you didn't live during the Soviet times. They did even more incredible things back then - they could make rivers flow backward! That's right! Digging holes in the ground was a piece of cake for them!
How can I get there grandfather, to the catacombs I mean? I really want to see it.
Oh, you're a smart young man, but why should I let you into the tunnel? My superiors strictly ordered me not to let anyone in without their knowledge.
What superiors grandfather? The Zone has been here for a long time - your superiors are long gone.
What do you mean, gone? Then who's making all that noise there?
Where?
In the tunnel. Every night there are noises. If it's not my superiors, then who?
Well, who knows what creatures might have settled there over the years. Do you want me to take a look?
Take a look? You thought you could deceive me son? It's alright, I suppose I owe you. Here's a spare key to my house, just don't touch anything inside!
Alright, grandfather. Thank you!
It's you! What else do you want from me?
What's wrong grandfather? Why are you so angry?
And he's mocking me too! Why did you rummage through my house? What were you looking for? Money, valuables? I have nothing except broken watches!
I wasn't looking for anything in your house grandfather! It wasn't me!
Then who rummaged through my cabinets? Ghosts? Even the key is gone.
What key?
The access key. The card I mean.
Is it the keycard to the door of the underground laboratory?
How do you know that? I knew it, you took it! Give it back!
It's a coincidence, I actually wanted to ask you for that keycard. I didn't steal your key grandfather, but I think I know who might have done it. I'm going to need the help of the trader.
Son, you find that scoundrel who rummaged through my cabinets without permission and please return my key. People have no shame or conscience anymore.
I'll take care of everything grandfather, don't worry.
What's interesting around here?

This is a rookie hangout. There's a trader named Sidorovich on the outskirts of the village. Beyond that is the military checkpoint - that's where you came from. On the other side of the checkpoint is the road going deep into of the Zone.
Tell me about the rookie village.

Well, what can I tell you about the local haven? Those who are a bit more experienced stock up on the essentials from Sidorovich and head to Garbage. More valuable artifacts can be found there and it's not too dangerous, you can gain some experience. Here, we have... idiots, to put it mildly. Sometimes miracle workers shows up with just a bag and nothing but slippers. He suddenly got a romantic impulse to visit, you know! Some kind of assistant professor who never held anything more serious than a logarithmic ruler in his hands. People like that don't last long here. Sometimes, experienced stalkers come by to give Sidorovich goods after a long raid and to get some rest for a day or two in a peaceful area.
Are the military bothering you? You seem to have settled in nicely here.

They don't really bother us. They have other things on their minds right now. They even say "Why waste ammunition on these stalkers when they'll die in anomalies anyway?" We don't provoke them either. When there are raids or inspections at the checkpoint from their headquarters, we hide in the basements. As you can see, we don't mess with each other.
Tell me about Sidorovich.

Sidorovich is the tight-fisted trader of Cordon. His main clients are rookie stalkers who have recently entered the Zone. He has many connections, including us. He has been involved in trading since childhood and used to sell soft drugs during his student years. He is both an authority and the subject of humor among stalkers. On one hand, he runs a fairly honest business and gives many newcomers a chance to survive in the Zone. On the other hand, he is criticized for his greed, selfishness, alcoholism, and mercantile nature. Long story short, everyone in the Zone knows him! He's the most influential trader around here. If Sidorovich can't get you something, no one can.
Tell me about yourself.

I'm sort of the leader around here. I teach the rookies and softies so that they don't get bumped off on their very first foray... Let me tell you, it's not an easy job! As for me, I've already trampled all over the Zone, left a chunk of my life here. I've been thinking of leaving, but it feels like forever since I arrived Cordon. There's some wicked witch casting spells I swear - I just can't seem to get out: either the military comes with a raid, or there's some damn emission. And honestly, money is a good incentive to stay too... Sometimes people offer very profitable jobs, so why not take them? You know how it is: you think, just one more expedition and that's it, I'm out - but it keeps happening over and over again.
Tell me about this place.
Tell me about this place.
Tell me about this place.

We're in Cordon. Beyond the military checkpoint is the outside world, and further on is the Zone. There's not much here that reminds you of the Zone: few anomalies, almost no artifacts, and you rarely encounter mutants. There's also not much noise here because the military tries to keep things in check. It's a rather quiet place, compared to the rest of the Zone of course.

We're in Cordon. Beyond the military checkpoint is the outside world, and further on is the Zone. There's not much here that reminds you of the Zone: few anomalies, almost no artifacts, and you rarely encounter mutants. There's also not much noise here because the military tries to keep things in check. It's a rather quiet place, compared to the rest of the Zone of course.

We're in Cordon. Beyond the military checkpoint is the outside world, and further on is the Zone. There's not much here that reminds you of the Zone: few anomalies, almost no artifacts, and you rarely encounter mutants. There's also not much noise here because the military tries to keep things in check. It's a rather quiet place, compared to the rest of the Zone of course.

This is the rookie village. There's a trader named Sidorovich on the outskirts of the village. Beyond that is the military checkpoint - that's where you came from. On the other side of the checkpoint is the road going deep into of the Zone.

This is the rookie village. There's a trader named Sidorovich on the outskirts of the village. Beyond that is the military checkpoint - that's where you came from. On the other side of the checkpoint is the road going deep into of the Zone.

This is the rookie village. There's a trader named Sidorovich on the outskirts of the village. Beyond that is the military checkpoint - that's where you came from. On the other side of the checkpoint is the road going deep into of the Zone.
Wolf, I've thought about your offer and decided to sell you that artifact.
You made the right decision. Sidorovich would have given you half as much for it. I know that miser well.
Yeah, I've already seen it for myself.
What's the job?

It's not a hard one, you just need to escort a guy to the stalker camp at the vehicle station. The client is new to the Zone but loaded, he's paying ten thousand. Half now - half when the job is done. Are you in?
Deal. Where's the client?
There he is, they call him Student. Once you get him to the base, come back, I'll give you the second half. Student will let me know when you reach the vehicle station.
Alright, I'm off.
No Wolf, sorry, I don't have time. Maybe someone else will take it?
Well, suit yourself. That kind of money doesn't grow on trees, I can always find someone willing to do it.
Good luck.
I have some free time now... I can escort that rookie.

I'm not surprised. Alright, he's standing over there, goes by the name Student. Once you get him to the vehicle station, come back to me, I'll give you the second half of the money. Student will inform me when he reaches the vehicle station.
Don't worry Wolf, I'll handle everything. The place isn't far away.
Too late, Shroud, too late. The rookie is already gone. You took too long to decide and missed an easy job.
Well, it wasn't something I really wanted anyway.
Job's done - Student is at the vehicle station.
So, everything went well? Nothing happened on the way?
Student happened... Man, he's quite the chatterbox. Now I understand why the payment for this job was so high.
Yeah, he loves to talk. What can you expect from an egghead? Speaking of the payment, here's the second part as promised.
Thanks Wolf. If anything else comes up, let me know.
I received your message. Is there anything interesting?
Of course there is! Give me your PDA, I'll send you the location of a stalker. His name is Leshiy, an experienced hunter. He eats bloodsuckers for breakfast you know? That's right!
What do I have to do? I'm not interested in bloodsuckers.

Well, you never know what you'll encounter in the Zone, Leshiy can teach you how to kill mutants more effectively. Don't refuse, not every rookie gets such a chance. So, are you willing to go hunting with Leshiy?
Well, why not! Send me the coordinates.
Now we're talking! I've sent it. Leshiy will be waiting for you down the road, not far from the vehicle station.
Thanks, Wolf.
I got your message. What do you need?
Good, you're here. I could handle this myself, but it'll be more fun with you. Plus, you can learn something.
How can I help?
I got myself a new detector and I want to test it. Let's go search for artifacts.
Artifacts? Those strange things that are highly valued on the mainland? Where can we find them?
Anomalies. More precisely, in anomalous fields. So, do you want me to teach you how to find artifacts?
Of course!
Alright then, follow me. It's not far from here.
Lead the way.
Which artifact did you manage to fish out? Let's see it.
Here, this is what I found!

You got lucky. Artifacts like this aren't often found around here. Well done - you're a capable student! If you want, I can buy this artifact from you at its full value. Traders will never give you the full price, but I happen to need this particular artifact. If you agree, I can also buy other artifacts from you, but only the rare ones. Think about it and if you decide to agree, come see me. Alright, let's head back to the camp. I bet you can't wait to tell everyone about your find, huh?
Of course. Let's go to the village, we'll talk about it there.

Alright, I found a path in the center of the field. Judging by the readings on my new detector, there's an artifact here, maybe even more than one. Now it's your turn. Here, take my old detector - it's an upgraded "Bear" model. With its help, you can not only determine the distance to the artifact but also the direction of the search. Throw bolts and bypass anomalies. When you see a signal on the screen, go in that direction. Just be careful! You won't immediately see the artifact. You'll need to get pretty close to it and then it will pop out. Good luck!
Thank you!

Here we are. See that pit behind me? That's the anomalous field. You need to be extremely careful here - one wrong step and you're done for. So, here's the plan: I'll investigate the anomaly, find a passage, and test the detector at the same time. Meanwhile, you watch me carefully, but don't forget to look around also - mutants might show up. Got it?
Got it.
Hello, Wolf. I wanted to ask you something. Do you happen to know where I can find Watchmaker? He's not at home, but I have a question for him.
He's left two days ago.
Where did he go?
To the Swamp, he's gathering some herbs there for his tinctures. Perfect for colds, I must say, helps with other ailments too.
Well, that's disappointing. When will he be back?

He'll be back today. How much time has passed anyway?... Hmm, he should have arrived by now. It's strange, he never runs late. There's a reason he's called Watchmaker. Maybe something happened to him? Listen Shroud, I have an important task for you: make your way to the tunnel not far from the barrier, Watchmaker was supposed to return through it. Who knows what might have happened there? He's a tough and experienced man, spent his whole life in the Zone, but age catches up with everyone, you know? I've sent you the coordinates. Now hurry.
I'm gone.
Do you have any work available?
I always have something interesting for you. Would you like to go on a safari?
I don't know, never been on one.

Would you like to? We don't have savannas here, no lions either, sorry. But we have boars that could give any lion a run for its money. It's those boars we need to calm down. A hunting group has just left a few minutes ago - they want to shoot mutants in the vicinity of the village. Would you like to join them?
Why not. There really are a lot of animals here, I still can't forget those boars near the "electric train" station.
Nice going my friend. It's good for the camp and some extra money won't hurt you. By the way, the pay for this job is 6,000 rubles. Not bad huh? So what do you say, will you go?
Do you even have to ask? Of course, I'll go! Give me the coordinates?
I've sent it to your PDA. Like I said, the guys just left, so there will be plenty for you to shoot at.
Alright, I'll go catch up.
Job's done, we shot those mutants good!
Already back? How was our local safari?
Great. I should do it again sometime.

Good, there are always plenty of creatures around here. I can't fathom where they all come from. Anyway, enough chit-chat - here's your reward. If there's any more work, I'll send you a message. Good luck!
Thank you, Wolf! Take care!
You don't have any customers right now, want to chat?
Sure thing. What's up?
Tell me about yourself.

Well, brother, back in the Union days I was involved in commerce! I was one of those... "cursed speculators." Got burned in a serious deal and paid the price. Served my time, made some acquaintances among the criminal folk. When I got out, the Soviet Union had already collapsed. Things didn't work out for me with the new order, so I had to do odd jobs for a while, ended up completely broke, and then bam - the Zone appeared. I came here and it's been a blast: almost everything is like in the old days! With my experience, trading here is just perfect.
Got it. Can you tell me something else?
Understood. Well, enough chit-chat.
What do you think about Logopedist?

I'll tell you this, Logopedist won't be around for much longer. I have a gut feeling that Koba will undercut him and take him down once and for all. The boss has been acting all high and mighty, thinking he's untouchable. He's cozying up to the snake, but doesn't realize that most of the guys can see through it. I'm not telling you this so you start blabbering left and right. I'm trusting you so you can trust me, right?. So, if you come across anything interesting, bring it to me. And remember: whoever comes out on top, Logopedist or Koba, I'll always be in my place regardless.
Tell me about Garbage.

No one used to go there before: trash, radiation... But then a couple of stalkers got lucky and dug up some valuable items, and that was it - people from all over the Zone rushed here like diseases to an old whore. Chaos ensued, but our guys restored order. The important thing is to have a strong hand and keep the scum in check and our guys did just that. Some sacrifices have to be made for order though, so the guys want their share and get a cut of everything. Ah, even though I'm doing pretty well here, I still want to reach places that are even more lucrative...
What's the latest news in the Zone?

There was an incident the other day... This stalker dropped by, pale as a ghost, hands trembling... He started confessing for some reason. Here's the story: He and his friend were escaping from poltergeists when suddenly a pack of snorks jump on them. Well, the guy freaked out, some crazy reflexes or something kick in and he sprayed his friend with bullets. Of course, he immediately realized what he had done and got the hell out of there. Meanwhile the snorks were already on the dead body. Why bother running after meat when there's already some on the ground, right? After that and the second bottle of vodka, his conscience kicks in, he starts howling all over Garbage: "Igor, you gave me your last medkit and I killed you, like a scum, killed you!.." Long story short, he howled and howled, and at night he hanged himself with a belt. It happens sometimes...
Oh, nothing, probably.
Whatever you say, it's up to you.
Can I help you with something?
No Shroud, thanks, but we handle things ourselves. There's something, but I doubt you'll accept...
What's the matter, Pathogenych? Come on, spill the beans or I won't stop bothering you. I'll decide whether I accept or not.

Well, not much to tell. There's a mutant that settled nearby, a pseudodog. It's a cunning bastard, comes to the camp and starts howling at night. It's worn out our nerves. We tried to find its lair a couple of times, but found nothing - no tracks, no sign of the creature, as if there was no one there. But it keeps howling at night.
You should deal with it at night, when it comes to the camp.

Ha, easier said than done. I'm telling you, these pseudodogs are smart bastards, and this one is particularly elusive. To go after a mutant during the night, you either have to be a complete idiot or Chuck Norris, and even then there's no guarantee you'll survive. Do you think you can handle it yourself? I'll give you a helmet with night vision. Heck, if you successfully kill it, you can keep the helmet for good, and I'll even throw in some extra cash. Deal?
Alright! I've been wanting to get myself some night vision goggles for a while now. Give me your helmet. I'll return it once I'm done.
No, thanks. I don't want to deal with pseudodogs.
Well, I said you wouldn't accept, didn't I? You can't handle it.
Whatever.
I took care of your mutt, Pathogenych. You were right, it's a cunning creature, hard to hit. But hey, I'm no pushover either. You won't hear howls at night anymore, I guarantee it.
That's fantastic, well done! Here's something extra from me, you've earned it. We'll finally get some decent sleep tonight thanks to you. Remember, you're always welcome here.
No problem, Pathogenych. If any other creature bothers you, you know who to call.
Do you have a job for me?

Yes, I have a job just for you. We need to find a technician who can fix weapons and gear for the boys. We'll pay him threefold for each repair and the boys will treat him with respect of course. You'll also get your share for the work. Are you up for it?
No problem.
Alright, just be quick about it.
I'm already on it.
I don't think so...
What nonsense... Get lost then.
Gone.
Hey boss, I found a technician for the crew, nice guy, and skilled too.
That's great. Now things will finally go back to normal around here.
Yeah, I'll see you later.
Listen Blockhead, would you be interested in repairing gear for the brothers?
I sure would. Besides, I'm not very good at expeditions as you know, but I can tinker in a way that would even make Van jealous. But hold on, Logopedist is offering jobs to Stalkers now?
Logopedist doesn't care if you're a Stalker. He promises payment for your work and the respect of the crew either way.
Then I agree.
Got it, I'll let Logopedist know.
The chimera is dead, here's the PDA.
Excellent, now, here's your new assignment: One of our servicemen, Private Nikita Sergeevich Nefedov, has deserted. It seems he fled into the Zone. Find out where he's hiding or confirm his death.
Alright, it should be easier than dealing with chimeras.
I found the deserter.
Where is he hiding?
Unfortunately he's dead. Here are the dog tags from the body.
Well, that's his fate. You and your partner are free to go now.
Goodbye, I hope to never see you again.
He's at the Bandit base, goes by the name Scar now.
So that's how it is. Alright, I'll send a squad to arrest him. You and your partner are free to go. Don't get caught again!
We'll do our best.
Listen Wolf, you're an experienced hunter. Can you give me some advice on dealing with a chimera?

You're not a bad hunter yourself. You took care of business with Leshiy and helped a lot with clearing the area of mutants. So, I'll tell you an unconventional method, a trick from one hunter to another.
Unconventional? What do you mean?
Do you know Trapper? Well, he made a repellent using the urine of a male chimera. If you cover yourself with it, the chimera should smell the scent of a rival and retreat. But it's not that simple...
As expected, what's the side effects?

The thing is, the urine affects each individual differently. Female chimeras will prefer to flee, a weak male will also run away, but if you encounter a strong male, the scent of a rival will drive him into a frenzy, and as you can imagine, an enraged male chimera will tear you apart.
Alright, do you have the repellent?
Here, take it.
Thanks.
I'd rather manage without it.
Wolf, have you seen a soldier named Nikita Nefedov around the village?
Soldiers don't come around here. We have tense relations with them.
Have there been any newcomers named Nikita recently?
There was a rookie named Nikita a while back. He had a strange nickname... something like "Porridge". I don't know why, but he moved to the vehicle station a while ago.
Thank you for the information.
Hello Pathogenych, I have a question for you. Have you come across a stalker named Porridge or Nikita? Supposed to be a former soldier?
No, I haven't met anyone named Porridge or Nikita. Besides, it's not common for stalkers to use their real names.
Well, maybe you've met a soldier recently?
How would you know whether someone was a soldier or not? Pick the one who jumps up the fastest when you yell "attention"?
Pathogenych, you're an experienced and observant stalker, can't you recognize someone who's served?
Well, there was one stalker called Epaulet, he had a scar on his face. He definitely looked like a former soldier, not just because of his nickname but also by how he looked at other stalkers.
Where is he now?
Black Stalker knows, he may have died or joined the Bandits.
Alright, thanks for the information.
Logopedist, do you know someone called Epaulet?
Epaulet? Isn't he one of the rookies?
Not exactly. He has a scar on his face and may have come here recently.
We had a guy with a scar on his face show up recently. Well, we just call him Scar.
Is he at the base now?
Yeah, look around if you need him.
Alright.
Good day, I have something to discuss with you.
What business could you have with me?
Nikita, I've been given a hard task, I can't handle it alone. I need a partner, can you help me out?
Well, if the business promises to be profitable... Wait, what did you call me?
I called you Nikita Nefedov, a former private in the Ukrainian army, now a deserter on the run. Kolyazin has gone crazy trying to find you.
Damn, my past won't let me go... Listen, I can't go back and Kolyazin won't rest until he finds me, help me out. Here are my dog tags, give them to him and tell him you found them on my dead body.
Alright, I'll do it.
Sidorovich, what's with the prices these days?
Some smartass stalker pissed off the soldiers, so Kolyazin raised the toll of delivering goods through the checkpoint. If I find out who that stalker was, I'll rip his head off.
Now what?
Well, tough times for all stalkers, what else? Now food, gear, ammunition, weapons, basically everything, has gone up in price. It's no longer profitable for me to trade here in Cordon.
Something needs to be done.
I don't know what the stalkers would do, but me, I'll be moving to the Dytiatky checkpoint if things don't change.
Got it.
Pathogenych, what's with the prices these days?
Kolyazin has gone mad, he raised the price for transporting goods through the checkpoint. That means increased prices of course.
Got it.
Antibiotic, what's with the prices these days?
Kolyazin snapped, he raised the price for transporting medical supplies through the checkpoint. That means increased prices of course.
Got it.
Tick, what's with the prices these days?
Kolyazin is a damn scumbag, he's demanding a fortune for transporting supplies through the checkpoint. I can't afford to be at a loss.
Got it.
Cousteau, what's with the prices these days?
Kolyazin has completely lost it, he raised the price for transporting goods through the checkpoint. Now it's tough with supplies and that means a price increase.
Got it.
What do you need?
I heard about Shmyga and how he pulled off a profitable deal. The problem is, he doesn't want to play fair. He only pays a tenth of the loot to the common fund.
Are you suggesting that I should get a share from Shmyga?
Very perceptive, but it's not just about getting a share, it's also about punishing him for his arrogance and unwillingness to play fair.
How should I punish him?
In a proper gangster way, draw some blood with a knife so that he thinks twice before crossing the line in the future. Tell him that if he wants to stay alive, he better cough up his share.
Alright, I'll do it.
Hand over Sidorovich's case, you snake.
What, don't you want the keys to my apartment as well? Back off while you still can.
Don't mess with me. It's not your case and you won't be able to open it anyway.
Don't worry, I'll find a way.
I'll shoot you right now, you bastard...
Are you sure talking tough at our base is wise? You have no chance of raising your gun to a real gangster.
We'll meet again.
Your boss sent me, you scum. You didn't play fair, didn't share the loot, the gang won't help you now.
The gang? Screw 'em, I'll have my own gang soon.
No, you won't. Logopedist ordered me to carve that smug smile right off your face.
What? We'll see who carves who, you sucker.
Go ahead and try.
Don't kill me! I'll give my share...
Well, maybe you should've started with that huh? I'll let Logopedist know.
Will you let me go?
You can live for now.
Not so fast, bastard. You owe Sidorovich, not Logopedist.
But you said it was my share for the common fund.
I lied! Now I'll not only take what you owe to Sidorovich, but your life too. Dead men don't talk after all...
I dealt with Shmyga.
Great, now no one will dare not to contribute to the common fund. Here's your reward.
Not bad, not bad.
Who do you think you are? I told you to cut him, not to kill him!
Yeah, I went too far, but it was hard to resist.
Do as you're told next time! What about the cash?
Not sure, Shmyga didn't have anything on him.
You really messed up this time. Get out of my sight before I order someone to get rid of you.
I'm already gone.
